   764→
   765→## Part 3: Values in Action
   766→
   767→### Instructions
   768→
   769→"The following scenarios describe common relationship situations. For each one, choose the response that best describes what you would typically do - not what you think you should do, but what you actually tend to do."
   770→
   771→### Items (8 scenarios)
   772→
   773→**Item 24: Honesty vs. Harmony**
   774→
   775→"When my partner does something that bothers me but mentioning it might cause conflict, I tend to:"
   776→
   777→| Option | Text | Coding |
   778→|--------|------|--------|
   779→| A | Say something right away, even if it's uncomfortable | honesty_high |
   780→| B | Wait for the right moment, then bring it up carefully | honesty_moderate |
   781→| C | Let it go unless it happens repeatedly | harmony_moderate |
   782→| D | Keep it to myself to avoid tension | avoidance |
   783→
   784→**Item 25: Autonomy vs. Connection**
   785→
   786→"When I need alone time but my partner wants to spend time together, I tend to:"
   787→
   788→| Option | Text | Coding |
   789→|--------|------|--------|
   790→| A | Take the space I need and trust they'll understand | autonomy_high |
   791→| B | Negotiate a compromise - some together, some apart | balanced |
   792→| C | Put aside my need and be present with them | connection_high |
   793→| D | Go along with togetherness but feel resentful | avoidance |
   794→
   795→**Item 26: Growth vs. Stability**
   796→
   797→"When there's an opportunity for something new but it would disrupt our routine, I tend to:"
   798→
   799→| Option | Text | Coding |
   800→|--------|------|--------|
   801→| A | Push for the new experience | growth_high |
   802→| B | Discuss it and weigh the trade-offs together | balanced |
   803→| C | Default to keeping things stable unless there's a strong reason to change | stability_high |
   804→| D | Avoid the decision as long as possible | avoidance |
   805→
   806→**Item 27: Authenticity vs. Accommodation**
   807→
   808→"When my partner has a strong opinion that differs from mine, I tend to:"
   809→
   810→| Option | Text | Coding |
   811→|--------|------|--------|
   812→| A | State my view clearly, even if we disagree | authenticity_high |
   813→| B | Share my perspective but remain open to theirs | balanced |
   814→| C | Listen to understand their view before sharing mine | connection_high |
   815→| D | Go along with their opinion to keep peace | avoidance |
   816→
   817→**Item 28: Intimacy vs. Self-Protection**
   818→
   819→"When my partner asks me to share something vulnerable, I tend to:"
   820→
   821→| Option | Text | Coding |
   822→|--------|------|--------|
   823→| A | Share openly, even if it's uncomfortable | intimacy_high |
   824→| B | Share gradually, testing how they respond | intimacy_moderate |
   825→| C | Share only what feels safe | protection_moderate |
   826→| D | Deflect or change the subject | avoidance |
   827→
   828→**Item 29: Playfulness vs. Seriousness**
   829→
   830→"When something goes wrong in a minor way (small mishap, forgotten task), I tend to:"
   831→
   832→| Option | Text | Coding |
   833→|--------|------|--------|
   834→| A | Laugh it off and find the humor | playfulness_high |
   835→| B | Keep it light while still addressing it | balanced |
   836→| C | Focus on fixing the problem | seriousness_high |
   837→| D | Feel annoyed even if I don't show it | reactivity |
   838→
   839→**Item 30: Service vs. Self-Care**
   840→
   841→"When my partner needs support but I'm already depleted, I tend to:"
   842→
   843→| Option | Text | Coding |
   844→|--------|------|--------|
   845→| A | Show up for them anyway | service_high |
   846→| B | Offer what I can while naming my limits | balanced |
   847→| C | Ask to help later when I have more capacity | selfcare_high |
   848→| D | Help but feel resentful about it | avoidance |
   849→
   850→**Item 31: Security vs. Honesty (Protective Lying)**
   851→
   852→"When telling the full truth might hurt my partner unnecessarily, I tend to:"
   853→
   854→| Option | Text | Coding |
   855→|--------|------|--------|
   856→| A | Tell the truth anyway - they deserve to know | honesty_high |
   857→| B | Tell the truth with care for how I deliver it | balanced |
   858→| C | Soften or omit details to protect their feelings | protection_moderate |
   859→| D | Avoid the conversation entirely | avoidance |
   860→
   861→## Scoring Algorithm
   862→
   863→```javascript
   864→function scoreValuesInstrument(responses) {
   865→  // responses object structure:
   866→  // {
   867→  //   part1Scaled: [20 numbers, 1-10],
   868→  //   part1Ranking: [5 domain IDs],
   869→  //   part2Open: [3 strings],
   870→  //   part3Scenarios: [8 option codes: 'A', 'B', 'C', or 'D']
   871→  // }
   872→  
   873→  const domainNames = [
   874→    'intimacy', 'honesty', 'growth', 'security', 'adventure',
   875→    'autonomy', 'family', 'service', 'playfulness', 'spirituality'
   876→  ];
   877→  
   878→  // Part 1: Domain scores
   879→  const domainScores = {};
   880→  domainNames.forEach((domain, index) => {
   881→    const importanceIndex = index * 2;
   882→    const accordanceIndex = index * 2 + 1;
   883→    
   884→    const importance = responses.part1Scaled[importanceIndex];
   885→    const accordance = responses.part1Scaled[accordanceIndex];
   886→    
   887→    domainScores[domain] = {
   888→      importance: importance,
   889→      accordance: accordance,
   890→      gap: importance - accordance
   891→    };
   892→  });
   893→  
   894→  // Top 5 ranking
   895→  const top5Values = responses.part1Ranking;
   896→  
   897→  // Part 2: Qualitative responses
   898→  const qualitativeResponses = {
   899→    partnerIdentity: responses.part2Open[0],
   900→    nonNegotiables: responses.part2Open[1],
   901→    aspirationalVision: responses.part2Open[2]
   902→  };
   903→  
   904→  // Part 3: Values in Action coding
   905→  const optionCoding = {
   906→    24: { A: 'honesty_high', B: 'honesty_moderate', C: 'harmony_moderate', D: 'avoidance' },
   907→    25: { A: 'autonomy_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'connection_high', D: 'avoidance' },
   908→    26: { A: 'growth_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'stability_high', D: 'avoidance' },
   909→    27: { A: 'authenticity_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'connection_high', D: 'avoidance' },
   910→    28: { A: 'intimacy_high', B: 'intimacy_moderate', C: 'protection_moderate', D: 'avoidance' },
   911→    29: { A: 'playfulness_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'seriousness_high', D: 'reactivity' },
   912→    30: { A: 'service_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'selfcare_high', D: 'avoidance' },
   913→    31: { A: 'honesty_high', B: 'balanced', C: 'protection_moderate', D: 'avoidance' }
   914→  };
   915→  
   916→  const actionResponses = {};
   917→  responses.part3Scenarios.forEach((option, index) => {
   918→    const itemNum = 24 + index;
   919→    actionResponses[itemNum] = optionCoding[itemNum][option];
   920→  });
   921→  
   922→  // Calculate avoidance tendency
   923→  const avoidanceCount = Object.values(actionResponses).filter(v => 
   924→    v === 'avoidance' || v === 'reactivity'
   925→  ).length;
   926→  const avoidanceTendency = avoidanceCount / 8;
   927→  
   928→  // Calculate balanced response tendency
   929→  const balancedCount = Object.values(actionResponses).filter(v => 
   930→    v === 'balanced' || v.includes('moderate')
   931→  ).length;
   932→  const balancedTendency = balancedCount / 8;
   933→  
   934→  // Identify high-gap domains
   935→  const highGapDomains = Object.entries(domainScores)
   936→    .filter(([domain, scores]) => scores.importance >= 7 && scores.gap >= 3)
   937→    .map(([domain]) => domain);
   938→  
   939→  return {
   940→    domainScores: domainScores,
   941→    top5Values: top5Values,
   942→    qualitativeResponses: qualitativeResponses,
   943→    actionResponses: actionResponses,
   944→    avoidanceTendency: Math.round(avoidanceTendency * 100) / 100,
   945→    balancedTendency: Math.round(balancedTendency * 100) / 100,
   946→    highGapDomains: highGapDomains
   947→  };
   948→}
   949→```
   950→
   951→## Interpretation Guidelines
   952→
   953→### Gap Scores
   954→
   955→| Gap | Interpretation |
   956→|-----|----------------|
   957→| 0-1 | Well-aligned |
   958→| 2-3 | Moderate gap |
   959→| 4-5 | Significant gap |
   960→| 6+ | Major gap - priority target |
   961→
   962→### Avoidance Tendency
   963→
   964→| Score | Interpretation |
   965→|-------|----------------|
   966→| 0.00-0.12 | Low avoidance |
   967→| 0.13-0.25 | Mild avoidance |
   968→| 0.26-0.37 | Moderate avoidance |
   969→| 0.38-0.50 | Elevated avoidance |
   970→| 0.51+ | High avoidance |
   971→
   972→---
   973→
   974→# 4. SSEIT: Schutte Self-Report Emotional Intelligence Test
   975→
   976→## Overview
   977→
   978→- **Purpose:** Measure emotional intelligence across four domains
   979→- **Items:** 33
   980→- **Subscales:** 4 (Perception, Managing Own, Managing Others, Utilization)
   981→- **Source:** Schutte et al. (1998) - Public domain for research
   982→- **Time:** ~8-10 minutes
   983→
   984→## Response Scale
   985→
   986→5-point Likert scale:
   987→```
   988→1 = Strongly Disagree
   989→2 = Disagree
   990→3 = Neither Agree nor Disagree
   991→4 = Agree
   992→5 = Strongly Agree
   993→```
   994→
   995→## Instructions
   996→
   997→"For each statement, indicate the degree to which you agree or disagree. There are no right or wrong answers. Please give the response that best describes you."
   998→
   999→## Items
  1000→
  1001→| # | Item | Subscale | Key |
  1002→|---|------|----------|-----|
  1003→| 1 | I know when to speak about my personal problems to others. | Perception | + |
  1004→| 2 | When I am faced with obstacles, I remember times I faced similar obstacles and overcame them. | Utilization | + |
  1005→| 3 | I expect that I will do well on most things I try. | Utilization | + |
  1006→| 4 | Other people find it easy to confide in me. | Managing Others | + |
  1007→| 5 | I find it hard to understand the non-verbal messages of other people. | Perception | − |
  1008→| 6 | Some of the major events of my life have led me to re-evaluate what is important and not important. | Perception | + |
  1009→| 7 | When my mood changes, I see new possibilities. | Utilization | + |
  1010→| 8 | Emotions are one of the things that make my life worth living. | Perception | + |
  1011→| 9 | I am aware of my emotions as I experience them. | Perception | + |
  1012→| 10 | I expect good things to happen. | Utilization | + |
  1013→| 11 | I like to share my emotions with others. | Managing Own | + |
  1014→| 12 | When I experience a positive emotion, I know how to make it last. | Managing Own | + |
  1015→| 13 | I arrange events others enjoy. | Managing Others | + |
  1016→| 14 | I seek out activities that make me happy. | Managing Own | + |
  1017→| 15 | I am aware of the non-verbal messages I send to others. | Perception | + |
  1018→| 16 | I present myself in a way that makes a good impression on others. | Managing Others | + |
  1019→| 17 | When I am in a positive mood, solving problems is easy for me. | Utilization | + |
  1020→| 18 | By looking at their facial expressions, I recognize the emotions people are experiencing. | Perception | + |
  1021→| 19 | I know why my emotions change. | Perception | + |
  1022→| 20 | When I am in a positive mood, I am able to come up with new ideas. | Utilization | + |
  1023→| 21 | I have control over my emotions. | Managing Own | + |
  1024→| 22 | I easily recognize my emotions as I experience them. | Perception | + |
  1025→| 23 | I motivate myself by imagining a good outcome to tasks I take on. | Utilization | + |
  1026→| 24 | I compliment others when they have done something well. | Managing Others | + |
  1027→| 25 | I am aware of the non-verbal messages other people send. | Perception | + |
  1028→| 26 | When another person tells me about an important event in his or her life, I almost feel as though I experienced this event myself. | Perception | + |
  1029→| 27 | When I feel a change in emotions, I tend to come up with new ideas. | Utilization | + |
  1030→| 28 | When I am faced with a challenge, I give up because I believe I will fail. | Utilization | − |
  1031→| 29 | I know what other people are feeling just by looking at them. | Perception | + |
  1032→| 30 | I help other people feel better when they are down. | Managing Others | + |
  1033→| 31 | I use good moods to help myself keep trying in the face of obstacles. | Utilization | + |
  1034→| 32 | I can tell how people are feeling by listening to the tone of their voice. | Perception | + |
  1035→| 33 | It is difficult for me to understand why people feel the way they do. | Perception | − |
  1036→
  1037→## Subscale Composition
  1038→
  1039→| Subscale | Items (1-indexed) | Item Count |
  1040→|----------|-------------------|------------|
  1041→| Perception of Emotion | 1, 5(R), 6, 8, 9, 15, 18, 19, 22, 25, 26, 29, 32, 33(R) | 14 |
  1042→| Managing Own Emotions | 11, 12, 14, 21 | 4 |
  1043→| Managing Others' Emotions | 4, 13, 16, 24, 30 | 5 |
  1044→| Utilization of Emotion | 2, 3, 7, 10, 17, 20, 23, 27, 28(R), 31 | 10 |
  1045→
  1046→## Scoring Algorithm
  1047→
  1048→```javascript
  1049→function scoreSSEIT(responses) {
  1050→  // responses = array of 33 integers (1-5)
  1051→  
  1052→  // Reverse-scored items (0-based indices)
  1053→  const reverseItems = [4, 27, 32]; // Items 5, 28, 33
  1054→  
  1055→  function reverseScore(score) {
  1056→    return 6 - score;
  1057→  }
  1058→  
  1059→  const scoredResponses = responses.map((r, i) => 
  1060→    reverseItems.includes(i) ? reverseScore(r) : r
  1061→  );
  1062→  
  1063→  // Define subscales (0-based indices)
  1064→  const subscales = {
  1065→    perception: [0, 4, 5, 7, 8, 14, 17, 18, 21, 24, 25, 28, 31, 32],
  1066→    managingOwn: [10, 11, 13, 20],
  1067→    managingOthers: [3, 12, 15, 23, 29],
  1068→    utilization: [1, 2, 6, 9, 16, 19, 22, 26, 27, 30]
  1069→  };
  1070→  
  1071→  const subscaleScores = {};
  1072→  for (const [scale, items] of Object.entries(subscales)) {
  1073→    const sum = items.reduce((s, i) => s + scoredResponses[i], 0);
  1074→    subscaleScores[scale] = {
  1075→      sum: sum,
  1076→      mean: Math.round((sum / items.length) * 100) / 100,
  1077→      itemCount: items.length
  1078→    };
  1079→  }
  1080→  
  1081→  const totalSum = scoredResponses.reduce((sum, r) => sum + r, 0);
  1082→  
  1083→  // Normalize to 0-100 scale
  1084→  const normalizedScores = {
  1085→    perception: Math.round((subscaleScores.perception.sum / (5 * 14)) * 100),
  1086→    managingOwn: Math.round((subscaleScores.managingOwn.sum / (5 * 4)) * 100),
  1087→    managingOthers: Math.round((subscaleScores.managingOthers.sum / (5 * 5)) * 100),
  1088→    utilization: Math.round((subscaleScores.utilization.sum / (5 * 10)) * 100),
  1089→    total: Math.round((totalSum / (5 * 33)) * 100)
  1090→  };
  1091→  
  1092→  return {
  1093→    totalScore: totalSum,
  1094→    totalMean: Math.round((totalSum / 33) * 100) / 100,
  1095→    totalNormalized: normalizedScores.total,
  1096→    subscaleScores: subscaleScores,
  1097→    subscaleNormalized: normalizedScores,
  1098→    rawResponses: responses
  1099→  };
  1100→}
  1101→```
  1102→
  1103→## Interpretation Thresholds
  1104→
  1105→| Normalized % | Level |
  1106→|--------------|-------|
  1107→| 20-40% | Low |
  1108→| 41-60% | Below Average |
  1109→| 61-75% | Average |
  1110→| 76-90% | Above Average |
  1111→| 91-100% | High |
  1112→
  1113→---
  1114→
  1115→# 5. DUTCH: Conflict Style
  1116→
  1117→## Overview
  1118→
  1119→- **Purpose:** Measure five conflict handling styles
  1120→- **Items:** 20
  1121→- **Subscales:** 5 (Yielding, Compromising, Forcing, Problem-Solving, Avoiding)
  1122→- **Source:** De Dreu et al. (2001) - Free for research use
  1123→- **Time:** ~5-7 minutes
  1124→
  1125→## Relationship Context Framing
  1126→
  1127→**Instructions Header:**
  1128→"The following statements describe different ways people handle disagreements and conflicts. Please respond thinking specifically about conflicts or disagreements with your romantic partner - not conflicts at work or with friends/family."
  1129→
  1130→## Response Scale
  1131→
  1132→5-point Likert scale:
  1133→```
  1134→1 = Never
  1135→2 = Rarely
  1136→3 = Sometimes
  1137→4 = Often
  1138→5 = Always
  1139→```
  1140→
  1141→## Instructions
  1142→
  1143→"When I have a conflict or disagreement with my partner, I typically..."
  1144→
  1145→## Items
  1146→
  1147→| # | Item | Subscale |
  1148→|---|------|----------|
  1149→| 1 | Give in to the wishes of my partner. | Yielding |
  1150→| 2 | Try to realize a middle-of-the-road solution. | Compromising |
  1151→| 3 | Push my own point of view. | Forcing |
  1152→| 4 | Examine issues until I find a solution that really satisfies me and my partner. | Problem-Solving |
  1153→| 5 | Avoid a confrontation about our differences. | Avoiding |
  1154→| 6 | Concur with my partner. | Yielding |
  1155→| 7 | Emphasize that we have to find a compromise solution. | Compromising |
  1156→| 8 | Search for gains. | Forcing |
  1157→| 9 | Stand for my own and the other's goals and interests. | Problem-Solving |
  1158→| 10 | Avoid differences of opinion as much as possible. | Avoiding |
  1159→| 11 | Try to accommodate my partner. | Yielding |
  1160→| 12 | Insist we both give in a little. | Compromising |
  1161→| 13 | Fight for a good outcome for myself. | Forcing |
  1162→| 14 | Examine ideas from both sides to find a mutually optimal solution. | Problem-Solving |
  1163→| 15 | Try to make differences seem less severe. | Avoiding |
  1164→| 16 | Adapt to my partner's goals and interests. | Yielding |
  1165→| 17 | Strive whenever possible towards a fifty-fifty compromise. | Compromising |
  1166→| 18 | Do everything to win. | Forcing |
  1167→| 19 | Work out a solution that serves my own as well as my partner's interests as well as possible. | Problem-Solving |
  1168→| 20 | Try to avoid a confrontation with my partner. | Avoiding |
  1169→
  1170→## Subscale Composition
  1171→
  1172→| Subscale | Items |
  1173→|----------|-------|
  1174→| Yielding | 1, 6, 11, 16 |
  1175→| Compromising | 2, 7, 12, 17 |
  1176→| Forcing | 3, 8, 13, 18 |
  1177→| Problem-Solving | 4, 9, 14, 19 |
  1178→| Avoiding | 5, 10, 15, 20 |
  1179→
  1180→## Scoring Algorithm
  1181→
  1182→```javascript
  1183→function scoreDUTCH(responses) {
  1184→  // responses = array of 20 integers (1-5)
  1185→  
  1186→  const subscales = {
  1187→    yielding: [0, 5, 10, 15],
  1188→    compromising: [1, 6, 11, 16],
  1189→    forcing: [2, 7, 12, 17],
  1190→    problemSolving: [3, 8, 13, 18],
  1191→    avoiding: [4, 9, 14, 19]
  1192→  };
  1193→  
  1194→  const subscaleScores = {};
  1195→  for (const [scale, items] of Object.entries(subscales)) {
  1196→    const sum = items.reduce((s, i) => s + responses[i], 0);
  1197→    subscaleScores[scale] = {
  1198→      sum: sum,
  1199→      mean: Math.round((sum / 4) * 100) / 100
  1200→    };
  1201→  }
  1202→  
  1203→  // Determine primary and secondary styles
  1204→  const sortedStyles = Object.entries(subscaleScores)
  1205→    .sort((a, b) => b[1].mean - a[1].mean);
  1206→  
  1207→  return {
  1208→    subscaleScores: subscaleScores,
  1209→    primaryStyle: sortedStyles[0][0],
  1210→    secondaryStyle: sortedStyles[1][0],
  1211→    rawResponses: responses
  1212→  };
  1213→}
  1214→```
  1215→
  1216→## Style Descriptions
  1217→
  1218→| Style | Description |
  1219→|-------|-------------|
  1220→| **Yielding** | Accommodating partner's needs; prioritizes relationship harmony over own goals |
  1221→| **Compromising** | Seeks middle ground; willing to give and take equally |
  1222→| **Forcing** | Advocates strongly for own position; competitive approach |
  1223→| **Problem-Solving** | Seeks win-win solutions; collaborative and creative |
  1224→| **Avoiding** | Sidesteps or postpones conflict; withdraws from disagreement |
  1225→
  1226→---
  1227→
  1228→# 6. DSI-R: Differentiation of Self Inventory - Revised
  1229→
  1230→## Overview
  1231→
  1232→- **Purpose:** Measure differentiation of self (Bowen family systems)
  1233→- **Items:** 46
  1234→- **Subscales:** 4 (Emotional Reactivity, I-Position, Emotional Cutoff, Fusion with Others)
  1235→- **Source:** Skowron & Schmitt (2003) - Free for research use
  1236→- **Time:** ~10-12 minutes
  1237→
  1238→## Response Scale
  1239→
  1240→6-point Likert scale:
  1241→```
  1242→1 = Not at all true of me
  1243→2 = Slightly true of me
  1244→3 = Somewhat true of me
  1245→4 = Moderately true of me
  1246→5 = Mostly true of me
  1247→6 = Very true of me
  1248→```
  1249→
  1250→## Instructions
  1251→
  1252→"These are questions concerning your thoughts and feelings about yourself and relationships with others. Please read each statement carefully and decide how much the statement is generally true of you on a 1 (not at all) to 6 (very) scale."
  1253→
  1254→## Items
  1255→
  1256→| # | Item | Subscale | Key |
  1257→|---|------|----------|-----|
  1258→| 1 | People have remarked that I'm overly emotional. | ER | + |
  1259→| 2 | I have difficulty expressing my feelings to people I care for. | IP | + |
  1260→| 3 | I often feel like I'm being controlled by others. | EC | + |
  1261→| 4 | I'm able to calmly listen to my partner, even when I disagree. | FO | − |
  1262→| 5 | I tend to remain pretty calm even when everyone around me is upset. | EC | − |
  1263→| 6 | At times my feelings get the best of me and I have trouble thinking clearly. | ER | + |
  1264→| 7 | When I'm with my partner, I often feel smothered. | IP | + |
  1265→| 8 | It's important for me to keep in touch with my parents regularly. | EC | + |
  1266→| 9 | When my partner criticizes me, it bothers me for days. | ER | + |
  1267→| 10 | I often agree with others just to appease them. | FO | + |
  1268→| 11 | I'm likely to be drawn into other people's problems. | EC | + |
  1269→| 12 | I usually do what I believe is right regardless of what others say. | IP | − |
  1270→| 13 | I want to live up to my parents' expectations. | EC | + |
  1271→| 14 | I'm very sensitive to being hurt by others. | ER | + |
  1272→| 15 | When I'm having an argument, I can keep focused on the issues and not let things get personal. | FO | − |
  1273→| 16 | At times, I feel as if I'm riding an emotional roller-coaster. | FO | + |
  1274→| 17 | I often feel overwhelmed. | ER | + |
  1275→| 18 | I'm good at knowing what I believe and stating my position clearly. | IP | − |
  1276→| 19 | I'm very uncomfortable when people express negative feelings toward me. | EC | + |
  1277→| 20 | Our relationship would be better if my partner would give me the space I need. | FO | + |
  1278→| 21 | I tend to react emotionally when things don't go as I've planned. | ER | + |
  1279→| 22 | I tend to distance myself when people get too close to me. | IP | + |
  1280→| 23 | I wish I weren't so emotional. | EC | + |
  1281→| 24 | It's hard for me to make decisions for myself when I know others might disapprove. | FO | + |
  1282→| 25 | I often end up taking care of others' needs at the expense of my own. | FO | + |
  1283→| 26 | I'm fairly calm even when things get chaotic around me. | ER | − |
  1284→| 27 | I'm able to say "no" to others even when I feel pressured by them. | IP | − |
  1285→| 28 | Our relationship has suffered because of my partner's unreasonable demands. | EC | + |
  1286→| 29 | When I am with others, I lose my sense of who I am. | FO | + |
  1287→| 30 | I often find myself making decisions based on what will make others happy. | ER | + |
  1288→| 31 | I often feel that my partner wants too much from me. | FO | + |
  1289→| 32 | I have a clear sense of who I am and what I believe. | IP | − |
  1290→| 33 | When one of my relationships becomes very intense, I feel the urge to run away from it. | EC | + |
  1291→| 34 | Relationships with others are usually more trouble than they're worth. | FO | + |
  1292→| 35 | When things go wrong, talking about them usually makes it worse. | ER | + |
  1293→| 36 | Sometimes I feel sick after arguing with my partner. | IP | + |
  1294→| 37 | My self-esteem really depends on how others think of me. | EC | + |
  1295→| 38 | I frequently feel threatened in close relationships. | FO | + |
  1296→| 39 | I feel things more intensely than others do. | ER | + |
  1297→| 40 | I tend to feel pretty stable under stress. | IP | − |
  1298→| 41 | I often wonder about the kind of impression I create. | EC | + |
  1299→| 42 | There's no point in getting upset about things I cannot change. | FO | − |
  1300→| 43 | I feel comfortable when people get close to me. | IP | − |
  1301→| 44 | I'm likely to smooth over or settle conflicts between two people I care about. | ER | + |
  1302→| 45 | I usually don't change my behavior simply to please another person. | IP | − |
  1303→| 46 | I'm uncomfortable being around people I don't know. | EC | + |
  1304→
  1305→## Subscale Composition
  1306→
  1307→| Subscale | Items | Description |
  1308→|----------|-------|-------------|
  1309→| Emotional Reactivity (ER) | 1, 6, 9, 14, 17, 21, 26(R), 30, 35, 39, 44 | Emotional flooding, reactivity to others |
  1310→| I-Position (IP) | 2, 7, 12(R), 18(R), 22, 27(R), 32(R), 36, 40(R), 43(R), 45(R) | Clear sense of self, stating beliefs |
  1311→| Emotional Cutoff (EC) | 3, 5(R), 8, 11, 13, 19, 23, 28, 33, 37, 41, 46 | Managing anxiety through distance |
  1312→| Fusion with Others (FO) | 4(R), 10, 15(R), 16, 20, 24, 25, 29, 31, 34, 38, 42(R) | Losing self in relationships |
  1313→
  1314→## Scoring Algorithm
  1315→
  1316→```javascript
  1317→function scoreDSIR(responses) {
  1318→  // responses = array of 46 integers (1-6)
  1319→  
  1320→  // Reverse-scored items (0-based)
  1321→  const reverseItems = [3, 4, 11, 14, 17, 25, 26, 31, 39, 41, 42, 44];
  1322→  
  1323→  function reverseScore(score) {
  1324→    return 7 - score;
  1325→  }
  1326→  
  1327→  const scoredResponses = responses.map((r, i) => 
  1328→    reverseItems.includes(i) ? reverseScore(r) : r
  1329→  );
  1330→  
  1331→  // After reverse scoring, ALL items are keyed so HIGH = LOW differentiation
  1332→  // We then reverse the subscale means so HIGH = HIGH differentiation
  1333→  
  1334→  const subscales = {
  1335→    emotionalReactivity: [0, 5, 8, 13, 16, 20, 25, 29, 34, 38, 43],
  1336→    iPosition: [1, 6, 11, 17, 21, 26, 31, 35, 39, 42, 44],
  1337→    emotionalCutoff: [2, 4, 7, 10, 12, 18, 22, 27, 32, 36, 40, 45],
  1338→    fusionWithOthers: [3, 9, 14, 15, 19, 23, 24, 28, 30, 33, 37, 41]
  1339→  };
  1340→  
  1341→  const subscaleScores = {};
  1342→  for (const [scale, items] of Object.entries(subscales)) {
  1343→    const sum = items.reduce((s, i) => s + scoredResponses[i], 0);
  1344→    // Reverse mean so higher = more differentiated
  1345→    const rawMean = sum / items.length;
  1346→    const reversedMean = 7 - rawMean;
  1347→    subscaleScores[scale] = {
  1348→      sum: sum,
  1349→      mean: Math.round(reversedMean * 100) / 100,
  1350→      itemCount: items.length
  1351→    };
  1352→  }
  1353→  
  1354→  // Total differentiation (average of subscale means)
  1355→  const totalMean = (subscaleScores.emotionalReactivity.mean + 
  1356→                     subscaleScores.iPosition.mean +
  1357→                     subscaleScores.emotionalCutoff.mean +
  1358→                     subscaleScores.fusionWithOthers.mean) / 4;
  1359→  
  1360→  // Normalize to 0-100
  1361→  function toNormalized(mean) {
  1362→    return Math.round(((mean - 1) / 5) * 100);
  1363→  }
  1364→  
  1365→  return {
  1366→    totalMean: Math.round(totalMean * 100) / 100,
  1367→    totalNormalized: toNormalized(totalMean),
  1368→    subscaleScores: subscaleScores,
  1369→    subscaleNormalized: {
  1370→      emotionalReactivity: toNormalized(subscaleScores.emotionalReactivity.mean),
  1371→      iPosition: toNormalized(subscaleScores.iPosition.mean),
  1372→      emotionalCutoff: toNormalized(subscaleScores.emotionalCutoff.mean),
  1373→      fusionWithOthers: toNormalized(subscaleScores.fusionWithOthers.mean)
  1374→    },
  1375→    rawResponses: responses
  1376→  };
  1377→}
  1378→```
  1379→
  1380→## Interpretation
  1381→
  1382→**After scoring, all subscales are oriented so HIGHER = MORE DIFFERENTIATED**
  1383→
  1384→| Normalized Score | Level |
  1385→|------------------|-------|
  1386→| 0-25 | Low differentiation |
  1387→| 26-40 | Below average |
  1388→| 41-60 | Average |
  1389→| 61-75 | Above average |
  1390→| 76-100 | High differentiation |
  1391→
  1392→---
  1393→
  1394→# 7. Data Schema Summary
  1395→
  1396→```typescript
  1397→interface UserAssessmentProfile {
  1398→  userId: string;
  1399→  completedAt: Date;
  1400→  
  1401→  attachment: {
  1402→    anxietyScore: number;        // 1-7
  1403→    avoidanceScore: number;      // 1-7
  1404→    attachmentStyle: string;
  1405→    rawResponses: number[];
  1406→  };
  1407→  
  1408→  personality: {
  1409→    domainScores: Record<string, { sum: number; mean: number }>;
  1410→    domainPercentiles: Record<string, number>;
  1411→    facetScores: Record<string, { sum: number; mean: number }>;
  1412→    facetPercentiles: Record<string, number>;
  1413→    rawResponses: number[];
  1414→  };
  1415→  
  1416→  values: {
  1417→    domainScores: Record<string, { importance: number; accordance: number; gap: number }>;
  1418→    top5Values: string[];
  1419→    qualitativeResponses: { partnerIdentity: string; nonNegotiables: string; aspirationalVision: string };
  1420→    actionResponses: Record<string, string>;
  1421→    avoidanceTendency: number;
  1422→    balancedTendency: number;
  1423→    highGapDomains: string[];
  1424→  };
  1425→  
  1426→  emotionalIntelligence: {
  1427→    totalScore: number;
  1428→    totalNormalized: number;
  1429→    subscaleScores: Record<string, { sum: number; mean: number }>;
  1430→    subscaleNormalized: Record<string, number>;
  1431→    rawResponses: number[];
  1432→  };
  1433→  
  1434→  conflictStyle: {
  1435→    subscaleScores: Record<string, { sum: number; mean: number }>;
  1436→    primaryStyle: string;
  1437→    secondaryStyle: string;
  1438→    rawResponses: number[];
  1439→  };
  1440→  
  1441→  differentiation: {
  1442→    totalMean: number;
  1443→    totalNormalized: number;
  1444→    subscaleScores: Record<string, { sum: number; mean: number }>;
  1445→    subscaleNormalized: Record<string, number>;
  1446→    rawResponses: number[];
  1447→  };
  1448→}
  1449→```
  1450→
  1451→---
  1452→
  1453→# 8. Administration Sequence
  1454→
  1455→| Order | Instrument | Items | Time |
  1456→|-------|------------|-------|------|
  1457→| 1 | ECR-R | 36 | 8-10 min |
  1458→| 2 | IPIP-NEO-120 | 120 | 20-25 min |
  1459→| 3 | Values | 29+3 open | 12-15 min |
  1460→| 4 | SSEIT | 33 | 8-10 min |
  1461→| 5 | DUTCH | 20 | 5-7 min |
  1462→| 6 | DSI-R | 46 | 10-12 min |
  1463→
  1464→**Total: 284 items + 3 open responses (~70-80 minutes)**
  1465→
  1466→---
  1467→
  1468→*Document Version 1.1 - Complete Item Specification*
  1469→*All items included - Ready for Claude Code implementation*
  1470→

content.content:
1→# Individual Portrait Report Design
     2→## Complete User-Facing Portrait Experience
     3→
     4→**Purpose:** Define the complete Portrait as users experience it - narrative flow, visual design, sample content  
     5→**Version:** 1.0  
     6→**Format:** Mobile-first scrolling document with expandable sections
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→# TABLE OF CONTENTS
    11→
    12→1. [Report Philosophy](#1-report-philosophy)
    13→2. [Report Structure](#2-report-structure)
    14→3. [Visual Design Language](#3-visual-design-language)
    15→4. [Complete Portrait Flow](#4-complete-portrait-flow)
    16→5. [Section-by-Section Design](#5-section-by-section-design)
    17→6. [Sample Complete Portrait](#6-sample-complete-portrait)
    18→7. [Interactive Elements](#7-interactive-elements)
    19→8. [Export Formats](#8-export-formats)
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→# 1. Report Philosophy
    24→
    25→## Design Principles
    26→
    27→### 1.1 Narrative Over Data
    28→
    29→The Portrait is not a test result. It's a story about who you are in relationships.
    30→
    31→```
    32→NOT THIS:
    33→"Your ECR-R Anxiety subscale score is 5.2 (high)."
    34→
    35→THIS:
    36→"You carry a deep capacity for connection and a keen attunement 
    37→to relationship dynamics. Your heart is oriented toward closeness."
    38→```
    39→
    40→### 1.2 Strength-Based Framing
    41→
    42→Every pattern is presented as an adaptive strategy that made sense, even when it now causes problems.
    43→
    44→```
    45→NOT THIS:
    46→"You have an anxious attachment style, which causes 
    47→clingy behavior and relationship problems."
    48→
    49→THIS:
    50→"When you sense distance, you move toward—seeking connection,
    51→wanting to resolve. This strategy developed because connection 
    52→matters deeply to you. The challenge is that it can sometimes 
    53→feel like pressure to your partner."
    54→```
    55→
    56→### 1.3 Actionable Insight
    57→
    58→Every insight connects to what the user can do differently.
    59→
    60→```
    61→Pattern → Cost → Invitation → Practice → Anchor
    62→```
    63→
    64→### 1.4 Paced Revelation
    65→
    66→Deep content is progressive. Surface first, depth on demand.
    67→
    68→```
    69→LAYER 1: Summary card (always visible)
    70→LAYER 2: Expanded explanation (tap to reveal)
    71→LAYER 3: Deepening questions (optional exploration)
    72→```
    73→
    74→---
    75→
    76→# 2. Report Structure
    77→
    78→## 2.1 Overall Architecture
    79→
    80→```
    81→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    82→│                     PORTRAIT STRUCTURE                           │
    83→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    84→│                                                                  │
    85→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
    86→│  │  COVER                                                     │  │
    87→│  │  Your name, date, personal touch                          │  │
    88→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
    89→│                           ↓                                      │
    90→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
    91→│  │  INTRODUCTION                                              │  │
    92→│  │  What this is, how to use it, what to expect              │  │
    93→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
    94→│                           ↓                                      │
    95→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
    96→│  │  SNAPSHOT                                                  │  │
    97→│  │  Visual overview of key dimensions                        │  │
    98→│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
    99→│                           ↓                                      │
   100→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
   101→│  │  THE FOUR LENSES                                          │  │
   102→│  │  ├── Attachment & Protection                              │  │
   103→│  │  ├── Parts & Polarities                                   │  │
   104→│  │  ├── Regulation & Window                                  │  │

   377→## 5.2 Introduction
   378→
   379→```
   380→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   381→│                                         │
   382→│  What This Is                           │
   383→│                                         │
   384→│  This Portrait is a map of your         │
   385→│  patterns in intimate relationships—    │
   386→│  how you protect yourself, what you     │
   387→│  long for, where you get stuck, and     │
   388→│  where you're invited to grow.          │
   389→│                                         │
   390→│  It's based on your responses to 284    │
   391→│  questions, interpreted through         │
   392→│  frameworks from attachment theory,     │
   393→│  emotion science, and couples therapy.  │
   394→│                                         │
   395→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   396→│                                         │
   397→│  How to Read It                         │
   398→│                                         │
   399→│  Take your time. This isn't meant to    │
   400→│  be consumed quickly. Some parts may    │
   401→│  feel deeply true. Others might need    │
   402→│  to sit with you before they land.      │
   403→│                                         │
   404→│  Nothing here is a diagnosis or a       │
   405→│  fixed truth about you. It's a          │
   406→│  snapshot of patterns—patterns that     │
   407→│  can shift as you grow.                 │
   408→│                                         │
   409→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   410→│                                         │
   411→│  A Note on Language                     │
   412→│                                         │
   413→│  You'll see words like "pattern,"       │
   414→│  "protective strategy," and "growth     │
   415→│  edge." These aren't judgments.         │
   416→│  Every pattern developed for a reason.  │
   417→│  Every strategy was once adaptive.      │
   418→│  Growth edges are invitations, not      │
   419→│  demands.                               │
   420→│                                         │
   421→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   422→```

   491→## 5.4 Attachment & Protection Lens
   492→
   493→```
   494→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   495→│  🔒                                     │
   496→│  Attachment & Protection                │
   497→│                                         │
   498→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   499→│                                         │
   500→│  Your attachment style is               │
   501→│  Anxious-Preoccupied                    │
   502→│                                         │
   503→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   504→│  │   Anxiety: 5.2      ████████████░░  ││
   505→│  │   Avoidance: 2.1    ████░░░░░░░░░░  ││
   506→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   507→│                                         │
   508→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   509→│                                         │
   510→│  WHAT THIS MEANS                        │
   511→│                                         │
   512→│  You carry a deep capacity for          │
   513→│  connection and a keen attunement to    │
   514→│  the emotional temperature of your      │
   515→│  relationships. Your heart is oriented  │
   516→│  toward closeness.                      │
   517→│                                         │
   518→│  At the same time, your pattern         │
   519→│  suggests that early experiences        │
   520→│  taught you to be vigilant about        │
   521→│  connection—to monitor for signs of     │
   522→│  distance, to worry about whether       │
   523→│  you matter, to feel the weight of      │
   524→│  uncertainty heavily.                   │
   525→│                                         │
   526→│  This isn't a flaw. It's a sensitivity  │
   527→│  that developed for good reason.        │
   528→│                                         │
   529→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   530→│                                         │
   531→│  YOUR PROTECTIVE STRATEGY               │
   532→│                                         │
   533→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   534→│  │                                     ││
   535→│  │  When you sense distance or         ││
   536→│  │  disconnection, you pursue.         ││
   537→│  │                                     ││
   538→│  │  You move toward—seeking            ││
   539→│  │  reassurance, wanting to talk it    ││
   540→│  │  through, trying to resolve the     ││
   541→│  │  uncertainty.                       ││
   542→│  │                                     ││
   543→│  │  This strategy makes sense. It's    ││
   544→│  │  trying to secure the connection,   ││
   545→│  │  to make the relationship safe      ││
   546→│  │  again.                             ││
   547→│  │                                     ││
   548→│  │  The cost is that your pursuit      ││
   549→│  │  can sometimes feel like pressure   ││
   550→│  │  to your partner—especially if      ││
   551→│  │  they need space to regulate.       ││
   552→│  │                                     ││
   553→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   554→│                                         │
   555→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   556→│                                         │
   557→│  PRIMARY & SECONDARY EMOTIONS           │
   558→│                                         │
   559→│  When you react in relationships,       │
   560→│  there's often a visible emotion on     │
   561→│  the surface and a deeper emotion       │
   562→│  underneath.                            │
   563→│                                         │
   564→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   565→│  │  SURFACE (what shows):              ││
   566→│  │  Frustration, criticism, urgency,   ││
   567→│  │  need to resolve NOW                ││
   568→│  │                                     ││
   569→│  │  UNDERNEATH (what's driving it):    ││
   570→│  │  Fear of abandonment, longing to    ││
   571→│  │  know you matter, terror of         ││
   572→│  │  disconnection                      ││
   573→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   574→│                                         │
   575→│  The work is learning to speak from     │
   576→│  the underneath—to say "I'm scared      │
   577→│  I don't matter" instead of "Why        │
   578→│  don't you ever listen to me?"          │
   579→│                                         │
   580→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   581→│                                         │
   582→│  YOUR TRIGGERS                          │
   583→│                                         │
   584→│  Based on your responses, these         │
   585→│  situations are likely to activate      │
   586→│  your attachment system:                │
   587→│                                         │
   588→│  • Partner seems distant, distracted,   │
   589→│    or preoccupied                       │
   590→│                                         │
   591→│  • Ambiguous or unclear communication   │
   592→│    ("we'll see," "I don't know")        │
   593→│                                         │
   594→│  • Perceived rejection or criticism     │
   595→│                                         │
   596→│  • Partner paying attention to others   │
   597→│                                         │
   598→│  • Waiting for response to text or      │
   599→│    bid for connection                   │
   600→│                                         │
   601→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   602→│                                         │
   603→│  WHAT YOU'RE REALLY SEEKING             │
   604→│                                         │
   605→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   606→│  │                                     ││
   607→│  │     "Reassurance that I matter.     ││
   608→│  │      Confirmation that we're okay.  ││
   609→│  │      Evidence that I won't be       ││
   610→│  │      abandoned."                    ││
   611→│  │                                     ││
   612→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   613→│                                         │
   614→│  This longing is valid. The growth      │
   615→│  edge is learning to hold the           │
   616→│  uncertainty while seeking              │
   617→│  reassurance in ways that invite        │
   618→│  your partner in rather than            │
   619→│  pushing them away.                     │
   620→│                                         │
   621→│                                         │
   622→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   623→│  │  ▼ DEEPER: Reflection Questions     ││
   624→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   625→│                                         │
   626→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   627→```
   628→
   629→## 5.5 Parts & Polarities Lens
   630→
   631→```

   635→│                                         │

   637→│                                         │
   638→│  You contain multitudes. Within you     │
   639→│  are different parts—some that          │
   640→│  protect, some that carry pain, some    │
   641→│  that long for connection. Sometimes    │
   642→│  they pull in opposite directions.      │
   643→│                                         │
   644→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   645→│                                         │
   646→│  YOUR DOMINANT PARTS                    │
   647→│                                         │
   648→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   649→│  │  THE VIGILANT PROTECTOR             ││
   650→│  │                                     ││
   651→│  │  This part watches for danger—      ││
   652→│  │  scanning for signs of              ││
   653→│  │  disconnection, rejection, or       ││
   654→│  │  abandonment. It's always on        ││
   655→│  │  alert, monitoring the              ││
   656→│  │  relationship temperature.          ││
   657→│  │                                     ││
   658→│  │  It protects you by: Catching       ││
   659→│  │  problems early, staying attuned    ││
   660→│  │                                     ││
   661→│  │  It costs you by: Creating          ││

   663→│  │  threats that aren't there          ││
   664→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   665→│                                         │
   666→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   667→│  │  THE PURSUER                        ││
   668→│  │                                     ││
   669→│  │  This part moves toward when        ││
   670→│  │  sensing distance. It wants to      ││
   671→│  │  talk, resolve, reconnect—now.      ││
   672→│  │  It can't rest until things are     ││
   673→│  │  settled.                           ││
   674→│  │                                     ││
   675→│  │  It protects you by: Seeking        ││

   677→│  │  fester                             ││
   678→│  │                                     ││
   679→│  │  It costs you by: Sometimes         ││
   680→│  │  overwhelming your partner,         ││
   681→│  │  escalating when de-escalation      ││
   682→│  │  would help                         ││
   683→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   684→│                                         │
   685→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   686→│                                         │
   687→│  YOUR KEY POLARITY                      │
   688→│                                         │
   689→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   690→│  │                                     ││
   691→│  │  LONGING FOR         FEAR OF        ││
   692→│  │  CLOSENESS    ←→     REJECTION      ││
   693→│  │                                     ││
   694→│  │  You deeply want     But you're     ││
   695→│  │  intimacy and        afraid that    ││
   696→│  │  connection          if you're      ││
   697→│  │                      truly seen,    ││

   699→│  │                      found          ││
   700→│  │                      wanting        ││
   701→│  │                                     ││
   702→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   703→│                                         │
   704→│  This polarity creates an internal      │

   710→│                                         │

   976→## 5.8 Negative Cycle Section
   977→
   978→```
   979→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   980→│  🔄                                     │
   981→│  Your Negative Cycle                    │
   982→│                                         │
   983→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   984→│                                         │
   985→│  Every couple has a negative cycle—     │
   986→│  a predictable dance they fall into     │
   987→│  when things get hard. Understanding    │
   988→│  your part helps you step out of it.    │
   989→│                                         │
   990→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
   991→│                                         │
   992→│  YOUR POSITION: PURSUER                 │
   993→│                                         │
   994→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   995→│  │                                     ││
   996→│  │        THE PURSUE-WITHDRAW          ││
   997→│  │             CYCLE                   ││
   998→│  │                                     ││
   999→│  │    ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────┐     ││
  1000→│  │    │   YOU   │ ──→ │ PARTNER │     ││
  1001→│  │    │ Pursue  │     │Withdraw │     ││
  1002→│  │    └────┬────┘     └────┬────┘     ││
  1003→│  │         │               │          ││
  1004→│  │         │   Escalates   │          ││
  1005→│  │         │      ↓        │          ││
  1006→│  │         └───────────────┘          ││
  1007→│  │                                     ││
  1008→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1009→│                                         │
  1010→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1011→│                                         │
  1012→│  YOUR MOVE                              │
  1013→│                                         │
  1014→│  When you sense distance or             │
  1015→│  disconnection, you move toward.        │
  1016→│  You want to:                           │
  1017→│                                         │
  1018→│  • Talk about what's happening          │
  1019→│  • Get reassurance that you're okay     │
  1020→│  • Resolve the issue NOW                │
  1021→│  • Understand what went wrong           │
  1022→│  • Reconnect and feel close again       │
  1023→│                                         │
  1024→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1025→│                                         │
  1026→│  WHAT'S UNDERNEATH                      │
  1027→│                                         │
  1028→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1029→│  │                                     ││
  1030→│  │  "I'm terrified that you're         ││
  1031→│  │  pulling away. That I don't         ││
  1032→│  │  matter to you. That this           ││
  1033→│  │  distance means we're not okay.     ││
  1034→│  │  I can't rest until I know          ││
  1035→│  │  we're connected."                  ││
  1036→│  │                                     ││
  1037→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1038→│                                         │
  1039→│  This is your primary emotion. It's     │
  1040→│  fear, not anger. It's longing, not     │
  1041→│  control. But it often shows up as      │
  1042→│  criticism or pressure.                 │
  1043→│                                         │
  1044→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1045→│                                         │
  1046→│  WHAT YOUR PARTNER EXPERIENCES          │
  1047→│                                         │
  1048→│  When you're in pursuit mode, your      │
  1049→│  partner likely experiences:            │
  1050→│                                         │
  1051→│  • Pressure to respond before ready     │
  1052→│  • Criticism for needing space          │
  1053→│  • Feeling like they can't do it right  │
  1054→│  • Overwhelm that makes them shut down  │
  1055→│    more                                 │
  1056→│                                         │
  1057→│  This creates the painful paradox:      │
  1058→│  the more you pursue, the more they     │
  1059→│  withdraw. The more they withdraw,      │
  1060→│  the more you pursue.                   │
  1061→│                                         │
  1062→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1063→│                                         │
  1064→│  YOUR TRIGGERS                          │
  1065→│                                         │
  1066→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1067→│  │  You're likely to start pursuing    ││
  1068→│  │  when:                              ││
  1069→│  │                                     ││
  1070→│  │  • Partner gets quiet or distant    ││
  1071→│  │  • An issue feels unresolved        ││
  1072→│  │  • You detect a shift in their mood ││
  1073→│  │  • They turn away (phone, work)     ││
  1074→│  │  • You feel criticized or rejected  ││
  1075→│  │  • Uncertainty about the            ││
  1076→│  │    relationship                     ││
  1077→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1078→│                                         │
  1079→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1080→│                                         │
  1081→│  BREAKING THE CYCLE                     │
  1082→│                                         │
  1083→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1084→│  │                                     ││
  1085→│  │  Your 10% move:                     ││
  1086→│  │                                     ││
  1087→│  │  When you feel the urge to pursue,  ││
  1088→│  │  pause. Say: "I'm feeling           ││
  1089→│  │  disconnected and I want to talk,   ││
  1090→│  │  but I can wait. Can we find a      ││
  1091→│  │  time that works for both of us?"   ││
  1092→│  │                                     ││
  1093→│  │  This acknowledges your need        ││
  1094→│  │  without demanding immediate        ││
  1095→│  │  resolution.                        ││
  1096→│  │                                     ││
  1097→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1098→│                                         │
  1099→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1100→│                                         │
  1101→│  REPAIR READINESS                       │
  1102→│                                         │
  1103→│  Signs you're ready to repair:          │
  1104→│  • Heart rate has slowed                │
  1105→│  • You can see their perspective        │
  1106→│  • You want to connect, not win         │
  1107→│  • You can own your part                │
  1108→│                                         │
  1109→│  Signs you're NOT ready:                │
  1110→│  • Still building the case              │

  1114→│                                         │

  1118→## 5.9 Growth Edges Section
  1119→
  1120→```
  1121→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1122→│  🌱                                     │
  1123→│  Your Growth Edges                      │
  1124→│                                         │
  1125→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1126→│                                         │
  1127→│  Growth edges are invitations, not      │
  1128→│  demands. They're the places where      │
  1129→│  trying something different—even        │
  1130→│  10% different—could create new         │
  1131→│  possibilities.                         │
  1132→│                                         │
  1133→│  ═══════════════════════════════════════│
  1134→│                                         │
  1135→│  GROWTH EDGE #1                         │
  1136→│                                         │
  1137→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1138→│  │                                     ││
  1139→│  │   Express needs directly before     ││
  1140→│  │   they become resentments           ││
  1141→│  │                                     ││
  1142→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1143→│                                         │
  1144→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1145→│                                         │
  1146→│  THE PATTERN                            │
  1147→│                                         │
  1148→│  You want connection deeply. But when   │
  1149→│  the moment comes to express a need     │
  1150→│  or want, something stops you. You      │
  1151→│  assess it as "not worth it" or "too    │
  1152→│  much" and swallow it instead.          │
  1153→│                                         │
  1154→│  The needs don't disappear. They        │
  1155→│  accumulate. What starts as small       │
  1156→│  disappointment becomes quiet           │
  1157→│  resentment becomes eventual eruption   │
  1158→│  or emotional withdrawal.               │
  1159→│                                         │
  1160→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1161→│                                         │
  1162→│  WHAT IT PROTECTS                       │
  1163→│                                         │
  1164→│  This pattern protects you from         │
  1165→│  rejection. If you don't ask, you       │
  1166→│  can't be told no. If you don't         │
  1167→│  express the need, you don't have       │
  1168→│  to face that it might not be met.      │
  1169→│                                         │
  1170→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1171→│                                         │
  1172→│  WHAT IT COSTS                          │
  1173→│                                         │
  1174→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1175→│  │                                     ││
  1176→│  │  TO YOU:                            ││
  1177→│  │  • Chronic low-grade resentment     ││
  1178→│  │  • Feeling unseen and unmet         ││
  1179→│  │  • Disconnection from your own      ││
  1180→│  │    wants                            ││
  1181→│  │                                     ││
  1182→│  │  TO THE RELATIONSHIP:               ││
  1183→│  │  • Partner doesn't know what you    ││
  1184→│  │    need                             ││
  1185→│  │  • Eruptions that seem to come      ││
  1186→│  │    from nowhere                     ││
  1187→│  │  • Erosion of intimacy over time    ││
  1188→│  │                                     ││
  1189→│  │  TO YOUR PARTNER:                   ││
  1190→│  │  • Can't succeed at meeting needs   ││
  1191→│  │    they don't know about            ││
  1192→│  │  • May feel blindsided by           ││
  1193→│  │    accumulated complaints           ││
  1194→│  │                                     ││
  1195→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1196→│                                         │
  1197→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1198→│                                         │
  1199→│  THE INVITATION                         │
  1200→│                                         │
  1201→│  Learn to treat your needs as           │
  1202→│  legitimate information for your        │
  1203→│  relationship, not impositions on       │
  1204→│  your partner. Share wants before       │
  1205→│  you've decided whether they're         │
  1206→│  "reasonable."                          │
  1207→│                                         │
  1208→│  This doesn't mean every need will      │
  1209→│  be met. But needs that are expressed   │
  1210→│  can be negotiated. Needs that are      │
  1211→│  hidden can only fester.                │
  1212→│                                         │
  1213→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1214→│                                         │
  1215→│  DAILY PRACTICE                         │
  1216→│                                         │
  1217→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1218→│  │                                     ││
  1219→│  │  Once today, express one small      ││
  1220→│  │  want, need, or preference—before   ││
  1221→│  │  you've decided whether it's        ││
  1222→│  │  reasonable or worth mentioning.    ││
  1223→│  │                                     ││
  1224→│  │  "I'd prefer..."                    ││
  1225→│  │  "I'd like..."                      ││
  1226→│  │  "It would mean a lot if..."        ││
  1227→│  │                                     ││
  1228→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1229→│                                         │
  1230→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1231→│                                         │
  1232→│  YOUR ANCHOR                            │
  1233→│                                         │
  1234→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1235→│  │                                     ││
  1236→│  │  "My needs are information,         ││
  1237→│  │   not imposition."                  ││
  1238→│  │                                     ││
  1239→│  │            [ Save Anchor ]          ││
  1240→│  │                                     ││
  1241→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1242→│                                         │
  1243→│  ═══════════════════════════════════════│
  1244→│                                         │
  1245→│  GROWTH EDGE #2                         │
  1246→│                                         │
  1247→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1248→│  │                                     ││
  1249→│  │   Tolerate uncertainty without      ││

  1526→│  │     at once. Let the insights       ││
  1527→│  │     settle. Notice what resonates.  ││
  1528→│  │     Notice what you resist.         ││
  1529→│  │                                     ││
  1530→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1531→│                                         │
  1532→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1533→│  │                                     ││
  1534→│  │  2. SAVE YOUR ANCHORS               ││
  1535→│  │     The anchors that resonate—      ││
  1536→│  │     save them. You'll need them     ││
  1537→│  │     when you're activated or        ││
  1538→│  │     shutting down.                  ││
  1539→│  │                                     ││
  1540→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1541→│                                         │
  1542→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1543→│  │                                     ││
  1544→│  │  3. TRY 10%                         ││
  1545→│  │     Pick one growth edge. Try       ││
  1546→│  │     something 10% different. Not    ││
  1547→│  │     a revolution—an experiment.     ││
  1548→│  │                                     ││
  1549→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1550→│                                         │
  1551→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1552→│  │                                     ││
  1553→│  │  4. USE YOUR AGENT                  ││
  1554→│  │     I'm here when you're in a       ││
  1555→│  │     hard moment, want to process    ││
  1556→│  │     something, or need to practice  ││
  1557→│  │     a skill. I know your Portrait.  ││
  1558→│  │                                     ││
  1559→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1560→│                                         │
  1561→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1562→│                                         │
  1563→│  WHEN TO SEEK MORE                      │
  1564→│                                         │
  1565→│  This app is support, not therapy.      │
  1566→│  Consider working with a therapist      │
  1567→│  if you're experiencing:                │
  1568→│                                         │
  1569→│  • Patterns you can't shift despite     │
  1570→│    consistent effort                    │
  1571→│  • Trauma that keeps affecting your     │
  1572→│    relationships                        │
  1573→│  • Depression, anxiety, or other        │
  1574→│    mental health concerns               │
  1575→│  • A relationship in serious distress   │
  1576→│  • Any thoughts of harming yourself     │
  1577→│    or others                            │
  1578→│                                         │
  1579→│         [ Find a Therapist ]            │
  1580→│                                         │
  1581→│  ─────────────────────────────────────  │
  1582→│                                         │
  1583→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
  1584→│  │                                     ││
  1585→│  │  Your patterns make sense. They     ││
  1586→│  │  developed to protect you. Now,     ││
  1587→│  │  with awareness and practice,       ││
  1588→│  │  you can choose something           ││
  1589→│  │  different.                         ││
  1590→│  │                                     ││
  1591→│  │  That's what growth edges are—      ││
  1592→│  │  not fixing what's broken, but      ││
  1593→│  │  expanding what's possible.         ││
  1594→│  │                                     ││
  1595→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1596→│                                         │
  1597→│                                         │
  1598→│         ┌─────────────────────┐         │
  1599→│         │   Go to Home        │         │
  1600→│         └─────────────────────┘         │
  1601→│                                         │
  1602→│         ┌─────────────────────┐         │
  1603→│         │   Start a Chat      │         │
  1604→│         └─────────────────────┘         │
  1605→│                                         │
  1606→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
  1607→```
  1608→
  1609→---
  1610→
  1611→# 6. Sample Complete Portrait
  1612→
  1613→## 6.1 User Profile (Sample)
  1614→
  1615→```
  1616→Name: Sarah

  1618→Personality: High Neuroticism, High Agreeableness, High Openness

  1626→
  1627→## 6.2 Portrait Snapshot (Sample)

  1633→# 7. Interactive Elements
  1634→

  1636→
  1637→```

  1639→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐

  1662→
  1663→## 7.2 Save Anchor Interaction
  1664→
  1665→```
  1666→TAP:
  1667→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1668→│                                         │

  1676→AFTER SAVE:

  1681→│                                         │

  1685→```
  1686→
  1687→## 7.3 Discuss with Agent Link
  1688→
  1689→```
  1690→At end of each major section:
  1691→
  1692→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1693→│                                         │
  1694→│  [ 💬 Discuss This with Agent ]         │
  1695→│                                         │

  1700→│                                         │

  1702→```
  1703→
  1704→## 7.4 Share Controls
  1705→
  1706→```
  1707→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐

  1717→│  │  [ ] Share "When I'm Shut Down"     ││
  1718→│  │  [ ] Share "The Most Important      ││
  1719→│  │       Thing"                        ││
  1720→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
  1721→│                                         │
  1722→│         ┌─────────────────────┐         │
  1723→│         │   Send to Partner   │         │

  1730→
  1731→---
  1732→
  1733→# 8. Export Formats
  1734→
  1735→## 8.1 PDF Export
