content.content:
1→# Dyadic Portrait Report Design
     2→## Combined Couples Portrait & Relational Dynamics
     3→
     4→**Purpose:** Define the combined Portrait experience when two partners have both completed assessments  
     5→**Version:** 1.0  
     6→**Prerequisite:** Both partners have individual Portraits + mutual sharing consent
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→# TABLE OF CONTENTS
    11→
    12→1. [Dyadic Portrait Philosophy](#1-dyadic-portrait-philosophy)
    13→2. [Prerequisites & Consent](#2-prerequisites--consent)
    14→3. [Report Structure](#3-report-structure)
    15→4. [Integration Algorithm](#4-integration-algorithm)
    16→5. [Section-by-Section Design](#5-section-by-section-design)
    17→6. [Interactive Features](#6-interactive-features)
    18→7. [Shared Agent Experience](#7-shared-agent-experience)
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→# 1. Dyadic Portrait Philosophy
    23→
    24→## 1.1 Core Principles
    25→
    26→### Both/And, Not Either/Or
    27→
    28→The Dyadic Portrait never takes sides. Both partners' experiences are valid.
    29→
    30→```
    31→NOT THIS:
    32→"Sarah's anxious attachment causes problems for Marcus."
    33→
    34→THIS:
    35→"Sarah's pursuit and Marcus's withdrawal interlock in a 
    36→predictable cycle. Both moves make sense. Both have costs."
    37→```
    38→
    39→### The Relationship as Third Entity
    40→
    41→The couple is more than two individuals.
    42→
    43→```
    44→Partner A (Individual Portrait)
    45→        +
    46→Partner B (Individual Portrait)
    47→        ↓
    48→The Relationship (Dyadic Portrait)
    49→- Shared patterns
    50→- Interlocking triggers
    51→- Mutual growth edges
    52→- Relational strengths
    53→```
    54→
    55→### Compassionate Understanding, Not Ammunition
    56→
    57→Every insight is framed to increase understanding, not to provide evidence in arguments.
    58→
    59→```
    60→WARNING THROUGHOUT:
    61→"This information is meant to help you understand and 
    62→support each other—not to win arguments or prove points."
    63→```
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→# 2. Prerequisites & Consent
    68→
    69→## 2.1 Requirements
    70→
    71→1. Both partners complete individual Portraits
    72→2. Partnership connected in app
    73→3. Both consent to Dyadic Portrait creation
    74→4. Both understand it's shared (no hidden sections)
    75→
    76→## 2.2 Consent Screen
    77→
    78→```
    79→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
    80→│                                         │
    81→│       Create Your Couples Portrait      │
    82→│                                         │
    83→│  You and [Partner] have both completed  │
    84→│  your individual Portraits. Create a    │
    85→│  Dyadic Portrait showing how your       │
    86→│  patterns interact.                     │
    87→│                                         │
    88→│  ⚠️ IMPORTANT                           │
    89→│  Both of you will see the same report.  │
    90→│  This is about mutual understanding—    │
    91→│  not proving points.                    │
    92→│                                         │
    93→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
    94→│  │  [ ] I understand this is shared    ││
    95→│  │  [ ] I'm doing this to understand   ││
    96→│  │  [ ] I can disconnect at any time   ││
    97→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
    98→│                                         │
    99→│         ┌─────────────────────┐         │
   100→│         │  Request Consent    │         │
   101→│         └─────────────────────┘         │
   102→│                                         │
   103→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   104→```
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→# 3. Report Structure
   109→
   110→```
   111→DYADIC PORTRAIT SECTIONS:
   112→
   113→1. COVER
   114→   Both names, date, "Your Couples Portrait"
   115→
   116→2. INTRODUCTION
   117→   Ground rules, how to read together
   118→
   119→3. YOUR ATTACHMENT DYNAMIC
   120→   How attachment styles interact
   121→   - Attachment map with both positions
   122→   - The core dynamic explained
   123→   - What each partner is seeking
   124→
   125→4. YOUR NEGATIVE CYCLE
   126→   The dance you fall into
   127→   - Full cycle visualization
   128→   - Each partner's moves and underneath
   129→   - Trigger interactions
   130→   - De-escalation map
   131→
   132→5. COMPATIBILITY INSIGHTS
   133→   Where you align, where you differ
   134→   - Values alignment
   135→   - Conflict style interaction
   136→   - Personality complementarity
   137→   - Regulation compatibility
   138→
   139→6. YOUR RELATIONAL STRENGTHS
   140→   What you do well together
   141→
   142→7. SHARED GROWTH EDGES
   143→   What you're both invited to work on
   144→   - Cycle-breaking edges
   145→   - Complementary individual edges
   146→
   147→8. COUPLE ANCHORS
   148→   Reminders for difficult moments
   149→
   150→9. REPAIR TOOLKIT
   151→   Scripts, rituals, practices
   152→
   153→10. CLOSING
   154→    How to use together, when to seek therapy
   155→```
   156→
   157→---
   158→
   159→# 4. Integration Algorithm
   160→
   161→## 4.1 Attachment Dynamic Classification
   162→
   163→```javascript
   164→function classifyAttachmentDynamic(partnerA, partnerB) {
   165→  const dynamics = {
   166→    'anxious-avoidant': {
   167→      name: 'The Pursue-Withdraw Dance',
   168→      description: 'One moves toward, one moves away',
   169→      intensity: 'high'
   170→    },
   171→    'anxious-anxious': {
   172→      name: 'The Intensity Spiral',
   173→      description: 'Both move toward with intensity',
   174→      intensity: 'very high'
   175→    },
   176→    'avoidant-avoidant': {
   177→      name: 'The Distance Dance',
   178→      description: 'Both move away when threatened',
   179→      intensity: 'low surface, high underneath'
   180→    },
   181→    'secure-anxious': {
   182→      name: 'The Anchor Dynamic',
   183→      description: 'One provides stability, one seeks reassurance',
   184→      intensity: 'moderate'
   185→    },
   186→    'secure-avoidant': {
   187→      name: 'The Patience Dynamic',
   188→      description: 'One pursues gently, one needs space',
   189→      intensity: 'moderate'
   190→    },
   191→    'secure-secure': {
   192→      name: 'The Collaborative Dynamic',
   193→      description: 'Both can regulate and repair',
   194→      intensity: 'low'
   195→    }
   196→  };
   197→  
   198→  return matchDynamic(partnerA.attachment, partnerB.attachment);
   199→}
   200→```
   201→
   202→## 4.2 Trigger Interaction Mapping
   203→
   204→```javascript
   205→function mapTriggerInteractions(triggersA, triggersB, positionA, positionB) {
   206→  const interactions = [];
   207→  
   208→  // Find where A's triggers are activated by B's moves
   209→  for (const triggerA of triggersA) {
   210→    const activatedBy = findActivatingMoves(triggerA, positionB);
   211→    if (activatedBy.length > 0) {
   212→      interactions.push({
   213→        triggered: 'A',
   214→        trigger: triggerA,
   215→        activatedBy: activatedBy,
   216→        responseFromA: positionA,
   217→        impactOnB: findImpact(positionA, triggersB)
   218→      });
   219→    }
   220→  }
   221→  
   222→  // Reciprocal for B
   223→  // ...
   224→  
   225→  return interactions;
   226→}
   227→```
   228→
   229→---
   230→
   231→# 5. Section-by-Section Design
   232→
   233→## 5.1 Cover
   234→
   235→```
   236→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   237→│                                         │
   238→│         [Interlocking pattern]          │
   239→│                                         │
   240→│         Your Couples Portrait           │
   241→│                                         │
   242→│        [Partner A] & [Partner B]        │
   243→│                                         │
   244→│            February 5, 2026             │
   245→│                                         │
   246→│              ↓ Read together            │
   247→│                                         │
   248→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   249→```
   250→
   251→## 5.2 Introduction
   252→
   253→```
   254→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   255→│                                         │
   256→│  Before You Begin                       │
   257→│                                         │
   258→│  GROUND RULES                           │
   259→│                                         │
   260→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   261→│  │                                     ││
   262→│  │  1. THIS IS FOR UNDERSTANDING       ││
   263→│  │     Not for winning arguments       ││
   264→│  │                                     ││
   265→│  │  2. BOTH PATTERNS ARE VALID         ││
   266→│  │     Your strategies both developed  ││
   267→│  │     for good reasons                ││
   268→│  │                                     ││
   269→│  │  3. THE CYCLE IS THE ENEMY          ││
   270→│  │     Not each other                  ││
   271→│  │                                     ││
   272→│  │  4. PAUSE WHEN ACTIVATED            ││
   273→│  │     If this triggers you, stop      ││
   274→│  │                                     ││
   275→│  │  5. READ TOGETHER                   ││
   276→│  │     This is designed to be shared   ││
   277→│  │                                     ││
   278→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   279→│                                         │
   280→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   281→```
   282→
   283→## 5.3 Attachment Dynamic
   284→
   285→```
   286→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   287→│                                         │
   288→│  Your Attachment Dynamic                │
   289→│                                         │
   290→│  THE PURSUE-WITHDRAW DANCE              │
   291→│                                         │
   292→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   293→│  │                                     ││
   294→│  │           ATTACHMENT MAP            ││
   295→│  │                                     ││
   296→│  │              Anxiety                ││
   297→│  │                 ↑                   ││
   298→│  │   Fearful      │      Anxious      ││
   299→│  │                │        ●S         ││
   300→│  │   ────────────+────────────        ││
   301→│  │                │   ●M              ││
   302→│  │   Avoidant    │      Secure       ││
   303→│  │                ↓                   ││
   304→│  │            Avoidance →             ││
   305→│  │                                     ││
   306→│  │   S = Sarah   M = Marcus           ││
   307→│  │                                     ││
   308→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   309→│                                         │
   310→│  THE CORE DYNAMIC                       │
   311→│                                         │
   312→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│
   313→│  │                                     ││
   314→│  │  When Sarah senses distance, she    ││
   315→│  │  moves toward—seeking connection.   ││
   316→│  │                                     ││
   317→│  │  When Marcus feels pressure, he     ││
   318→│  │  moves away—seeking space.          ││
   319→│  │                                     ││
   320→│  │  Sarah's pursuit triggers Marcus's  ││
   321→│  │  withdrawal. Marcus's withdrawal    ││
   322→│  │  triggers Sarah's pursuit.          ││
   323→│  │                                     ││
   324→│  │  Neither is wrong. Both are         ││
   325→│  │  protecting themselves. The cycle   ││
   326→│  │  is the problem.                    ││
   327→│  │                                     ││
   328→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   329→│                                         │
   330→│  WHAT YOU'RE EACH SEEKING               │
   331→│                                         │
   332→│  ┌──────────────────┐┌─────────────────┐│
   333→│  │ SARAH            ││ MARCUS          ││
   334→│  │                  ││                 ││
   335→│  │ Reassurance      ││ Space to        ││
   336→│  │ that she matters ││ regulate        ││
   337→│  │                  ││                 ││
   338→│  │ Confirmation     ││ Time to think   ││
   339→│  │ you're not       ││ without         ││
   340→│  │ leaving          ││ pressure        ││
   341→│  └──────────────────┘└─────────────────┘│
   342→│                                         │
   343→│  Both needs are valid. The challenge    │
   344→│  is that your strategies for meeting    │
   345→│  them inadvertently threaten each       │
   346→│  other's needs.                         │
   347→│                                         │
   348→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘

   772→│  │  Portrait. I can help you:          ││
   773→│  │                                     ││
   774→│  │  • Process a recent conflict        ││
   775→│  │  • Practice repair                  ││

   778→│  │  Both of you will see this chat.    ││
   779→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│
   780→│                                         │
   781→│  QUICK STARTS                           │
   782→│                                         │
   783→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐│

   785→│  └─────────────────────────────────────┘│

   787→│  │  🛠️ Help us practice repair         ││

   795→
   796→## 7.2 Agent Directives in Couple Mode
   797→

   799→const coupleAgentDirectives = [

   807→];
   808→```

content.content:
1→# Database Schema Specification
     2→## Couples Relationship App - Data Persistence Design
     3→
     4→**Purpose:** Define complete data model, relationships, and persistence strategy  
     5→**Version:** 1.0  
     6→**Database:** PostgreSQL (primary) + Redis (caching/sessions)  
     7→**ORM Recommendation:** Prisma or TypeORM
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→# TABLE OF CONTENTS
    12→
    13→1. [Overview](#1-overview)
    14→2. [Entity Relationship Diagram](#2-entity-relationship-diagram)
    15→3. [Core Entities](#3-core-entities)
    16→4. [Assessment Entities](#4-assessment-entities)
    17→5. [Portrait Entities](#5-portrait-entities)
    18→6. [Agent & Session Entities](#6-agent--session-entities)
    19→7. [Partner & Sharing Entities](#7-partner--sharing-entities)
    20→8. [Progress & Growth Entities](#8-progress--growth-entities)
    21→9. [Indexes & Performance](#9-indexes--performance)
    22→10. [Data Lifecycle & Retention](#10-data-lifecycle--retention)
    23→11. [Security & Encryption](#11-security--encryption)
    24→12. [Migration Strategy](#12-migration-strategy)
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→# 1. Overview
    29→
    30→## Design Principles
    31→
    32→1. **Privacy by Default** - Sensitive data encrypted at rest, minimal exposure
    33→2. **Consent-Driven Sharing** - Partner access requires explicit grants
    34→3. **Audit Trail** - Track data access and modifications
    35→4. **Soft Deletes** - Allow recovery, support GDPR/CCPA requests
    36→5. **Denormalization for Performance** - Cache computed values where appropriate
    37→6. **Versioning** - Portrait and assessment versions tracked
    38→
    39→## Database Selection Rationale
    40→
    41→| Component | Technology | Rationale |
    42→|-----------|------------|-----------|
    43→| Primary | PostgreSQL | ACID compliance, JSON support, full-text search |
    44→| Cache | Redis | Session storage, rate limiting, real-time features |
    45→| Search | PostgreSQL FTS | Conversation search, pattern matching |
    46→| Files | S3-compatible | Avatar images, exports |
    47→
    48→## Schema Conventions
    49→
    50→```
    51→NAMING:
    52→- Tables: snake_case, plural (users, assessments)
    53→- Columns: snake_case (created_at, user_id)
    54→- Primary Keys: id (UUID)
    55→- Foreign Keys: {table_singular}_id (user_id, portrait_id)
    56→- Timestamps: created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
    57→
    58→TYPES:
    59→- IDs: UUID v4
    60→- Timestamps: TIMESTAMPTZ
    61→- Money: DECIMAL(10,2)
    62→- JSON: JSONB
    63→- Enums: PostgreSQL ENUM types
    64→```
    65→
    66→---
    67→
    68→# 2. Entity Relationship Diagram
    69→
    70→```
    71→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    72→│                              ENTITY RELATIONSHIPS                                │
    73→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    74→│                                                                                  │
    75→│  ┌──────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐        │
    76→│  │  users   │────────▶│ user_preferences │         │  partnerships    │        │
    77→│  └────┬─────┘         └──────────────────┘         └────────┬─────────┘        │
    78→│       │                                                      │                  │
    79→│       │ 1:N                                            M:N   │                  │
    80→│       ▼                                                      │                  │
    81→│  ┌──────────────┐                                           │                  │
    82→│  │ assessments  │◀──────────────────────────────────────────┘                  │
    83→│  └────┬─────────┘                                                              │
    84→│       │                                                                         │
    85→│       │ 1:N                                                                     │
    86→│       ▼                                                                         │
    87→│  ┌──────────────────┐                                                          │
    88→│  │assessment_responses│                                                         │
    89→│  └──────────────────┘                                                          │
    90→│       │                                                                         │
    91→│       │ (computed from)                                                         │
    92→│       ▼                                                                         │
    93→│  ┌──────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐        │
    94→│  │ portraits│────────▶│ portrait_lenses  │         │ portrait_versions│        │
    95→│  └────┬─────┘         └──────────────────┘         └──────────────────┘        │
    96→│       │                                                                         │
    97→│       │ 1:N                                                                     │
    98→│       ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐                     │
    99→│       │                                                   │                     │
   100→│       ▼                                                   ▼                     │
   101→│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐             │
   102→│  │ growth_edges │    │anchor_points │    │ negative_cycle_config│             │
   103→│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────────────┘             │
   104→│                                                                                 │
   105→│                                                                                 │
   106→│  ┌──────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐        │
   107→│  │ sessions │────────▶│    messages      │────────▶│ message_metadata │        │
   108→│  └──────────┘         └──────────────────┘         └──────────────────┘        │
   109→│       │                                                                         │
   110→│       │ 1:1                                                                     │
   111→│       ▼                                                                         │
   112→│  ┌──────────────────┐                                                          │
   113→│  │ session_states   │                                                          │
   114→│  └──────────────────┘                                                          │
   115→│                                                                                 │
   116→│                                                                                 │
   117→│  ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐                             │
   118→│  │ growth_tracking  │────────▶│    milestones    │                             │
   119→│  └──────────────────┘         └──────────────────┘                             │
   120→│                                                                                 │
   121→│                                                                                 │
   122→│  ┌──────────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐                             │
   123→│  │ partner_shares   │────────▶│  share_access_log│                             │
   124→│  └──────────────────┘         └──────────────────┘                             │
   125→│                                                                                 │
   126→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   127→```
   128→
   129→---
   130→
   131→# 3. Core Entities
   132→
   133→## 3.1 Users
   134→
   135→```sql
   136→CREATE TYPE user_status AS ENUM ('active', 'suspended', 'deleted');
   137→CREATE TYPE auth_provider AS ENUM ('email', 'google', 'apple');
   138→
   139→CREATE TABLE users (
   140→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   141→    
   142→    -- Identity
   143→    email               VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   144→    email_verified      BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   145→    phone               VARCHAR(20),
   146→    phone_verified      BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   147→    
   148→    -- Profile
   149→    first_name          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   150→    last_name           VARCHAR(100),
   151→    display_name        VARCHAR(100),
   152→    avatar_url          VARCHAR(500),
   153→    timezone            VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'UTC',
   154→    locale              VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en-US',
   155→    
   156→    -- Authentication
   157→    password_hash       VARCHAR(255),  -- NULL if OAuth only
   158→    auth_provider       auth_provider NOT NULL DEFAULT 'email',
   159→    auth_provider_id    VARCHAR(255),  -- External ID from OAuth provider
   160→    
   161→    -- Status
   162→    status              user_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
   163→    last_active_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
   164→    
   165→    -- Onboarding
   166→    onboarding_completed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   167→    onboarding_step     VARCHAR(50),
   168→    
   169→    -- Timestamps
   170→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   171→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   172→    deleted_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
   173→    
   174→    -- Constraints
   175→    CONSTRAINT email_format CHECK (email ~* '^[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}$')
   176→);
   177→
   178→-- Indexes
   179→CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
   180→CREATE INDEX idx_users_status ON users(status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
   181→CREATE INDEX idx_users_auth_provider ON users(auth_provider, auth_provider_id);
   182→```
   183→
   184→## 3.2 User Preferences
   185→
   186→```sql
   187→CREATE TABLE user_preferences (
   188→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   189→    user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   190→    
   191→    -- Notification Preferences
   192→    notifications_enabled       BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   193→    push_enabled               BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   194→    email_digest_enabled       BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   195→    email_digest_frequency     VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'weekly',
   196→    daily_checkin_reminder     BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   197→    daily_checkin_time         TIME,
   198→    
   199→    -- App Preferences
   200→    theme                      VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'system',
   201→    reduce_motion              BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   202→    large_text                 BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   203→    
   204→    -- Privacy Preferences
   205→    analytics_enabled          BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   206→    crash_reporting_enabled    BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   207→    
   208→    -- Assessment Preferences
   209→    assessment_auto_advance    BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   210→    
   211→    -- Timestamps
   212→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   213→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   214→    
   215→    CONSTRAINT unique_user_preferences UNIQUE (user_id)
   216→);
   217→```
   218→
   219→## 3.3 Authentication & Sessions
   220→
   221→```sql
   222→CREATE TABLE auth_tokens (
   223→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   224→    user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   225→    
   226→    -- Token Data
   227→    token_hash          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,  -- Hashed token
   228→    token_type          VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,   -- 'access', 'refresh', 'reset', 'verify'
   229→    
   230→    -- Metadata
   231→    device_info         JSONB,
   232→    ip_address          INET,
   233→    user_agent          TEXT,
   234→    
   235→    -- Expiry
   236→    expires_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
   237→    revoked_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
   238→    
   239→    -- Timestamps
   240→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   241→    
   242→    CONSTRAINT valid_token_type CHECK (token_type IN ('access', 'refresh', 'reset', 'verify'))
   243→);
   244→
   245→CREATE INDEX idx_auth_tokens_user ON auth_tokens(user_id);
   246→CREATE INDEX idx_auth_tokens_hash ON auth_tokens(token_hash) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL;
   247→CREATE INDEX idx_auth_tokens_expiry ON auth_tokens(expires_at) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL;
   248→```
   249→
   250→---
   251→
   252→# 4. Assessment Entities
   253→
   254→## 4.1 Assessments
   255→
   256→```sql
   257→CREATE TYPE assessment_status AS ENUM (
   258→    'not_started',
   259→    'in_progress', 
   260→    'completed',
   261→    'abandoned'
   262→);
   263→
   264→CREATE TABLE assessments (
   265→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   266→    user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   267→    
   268→    -- Status
   269→    status              assessment_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'not_started',
   270→    
   271→    -- Progress Tracking
   272→    current_section     VARCHAR(50),  -- 'attachment', 'personality', etc.
   273→    current_question    INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   274→    sections_completed  VARCHAR(50)[] DEFAULT '{}',
   275→    
   276→    -- Timing
   277→    started_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
   278→    completed_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
   279→    last_activity_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
   280→    total_time_seconds  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   281→    
   282→    -- Version (for instrument updates)
   283→    instrument_version  VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT '1.0',
   284→    
   285→    -- Computed Scores (denormalized for quick access)
   286→    scores              JSONB,  -- Full computed scores after completion
   287→    
   288→    -- Timestamps
   289→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   290→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   291→    
   292→    CONSTRAINT valid_sections CHECK (
   293→        sections_completed <@ ARRAY['attachment', 'personality', 'values', 
   294→                                    'emotional_intelligence', 'conflict_style', 
   295→                                    'differentiation']::VARCHAR(50)[]
   296→    )
   297→);
   298→
   299→CREATE INDEX idx_assessments_user ON assessments(user_id);
   300→CREATE INDEX idx_assessments_status ON assessments(user_id, status);
   301→CREATE INDEX idx_assessments_completed ON assessments(user_id, completed_at DESC);
   302→```
   303→
   304→## 4.2 Assessment Responses
   305→
   306→```sql
   307→CREATE TABLE assessment_responses (
   308→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   309→    assessment_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES assessments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   310→    
   311→    -- Question Identification
   312→    section             VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   313→    question_id         VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'ECR_1', 'IPIP_45'
   314→    question_index      INTEGER NOT NULL,
   315→    
   316→    -- Response Data
   317→    response_type       VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'likert', 'text', 'ranking', 'choice'
   318→    response_value      JSONB NOT NULL,        -- Flexible for different types
   319→    
   320→    -- Timing
   321→    response_time_ms    INTEGER,  -- Time to answer
   322→    
   323→    -- Timestamps
   324→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   325→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   326→    
   327→    -- Constraints
   328→    CONSTRAINT unique_response UNIQUE (assessment_id, section, question_id),
   329→    CONSTRAINT valid_section CHECK (
   330→        section IN ('attachment', 'personality', 'values', 
   331→                   'emotional_intelligence', 'conflict_style', 'differentiation')
   332→    )
   333→);
   334→
   335→CREATE INDEX idx_responses_assessment ON assessment_responses(assessment_id);
   336→CREATE INDEX idx_responses_section ON assessment_responses(assessment_id, section);
   337→
   338→-- Example response_value structures:
   339→-- Likert: {"value": 5}
   340→-- Text: {"text": "I want to be patient and understanding..."}
   341→-- Ranking: {"ranked": ["honesty", "intimacy", "growth", "security", "playfulness"]}
   342→-- Choice: {"selected": "B"}
   343→```
   344→
   345→## 4.3 Assessment Section Scores
   346→
   347→```sql
   348→CREATE TABLE assessment_section_scores (
   349→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   350→    assessment_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES assessments(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   351→    
   352→    -- Section Identification
   353→    section             VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   354→    
   355→    -- Scores (structure varies by instrument)
   356→    raw_scores          JSONB NOT NULL,
   357→    normalized_scores   JSONB NOT NULL,
   358→    subscale_scores     JSONB,
   359→    percentiles         JSONB,
   360→    
   361→    -- Interpretations
   362→    primary_classification VARCHAR(100),  -- e.g., 'anxious-preoccupied'
   363→    secondary_classification VARCHAR(100),
   364→    
   365→    -- Computation Metadata
   366→    computed_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   367→    algorithm_version   VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
   368→    
   369→    CONSTRAINT unique_section_score UNIQUE (assessment_id, section)
   370→);
   371→
   372→CREATE INDEX idx_section_scores_assessment ON assessment_section_scores(assessment_id);
   373→```
   374→
   375→---
   376→
   377→# 5. Portrait Entities
   378→
   379→## 5.1 Portraits
   380→
   381→```sql
   382→CREATE TYPE portrait_status AS ENUM (
   383→    'generating',
   384→    'active',
   385→    'superseded',
   386→    'deleted'
   387→);
   388→
   389→CREATE TABLE portraits (
   390→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   391→    user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   392→    assessment_id       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES assessments(id),
   393→    
   394→    -- Status
   395→    status              portrait_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'generating',
   396→    version             INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   397→    
   398→    -- Snapshot Data (denormalized for quick display)
   399→    snapshot            JSONB NOT NULL,
   400→    
   401→    -- Full Portrait Data
   402→    narrative           JSONB,  -- Synthesized narrative sections
   403→    
   404→    -- Generation Metadata
   405→    generated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
   406→    generation_time_ms  INTEGER,
   407→    algorithm_version   VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
   408→    
   409→    -- Timestamps
   410→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   411→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   412→    
   413→    CONSTRAINT one_active_portrait_per_user UNIQUE (user_id, status) 
   414→        WHERE status = 'active'
   415→);
   416→
   417→CREATE INDEX idx_portraits_user ON portraits(user_id);
   418→CREATE INDEX idx_portraits_active ON portraits(user_id) WHERE status = 'active';
   419→CREATE INDEX idx_portraits_assessment ON portraits(assessment_id);
   420→```
   421→
   422→## 5.2 Portrait Lenses
   423→
   424→```sql
   425→CREATE TYPE lens_type AS ENUM (
   426→    'attachment_protection',
   427→    'parts_polarities',
   428→    'regulation_window',
   429→    'values_becoming'
   430→);
   431→
   432→CREATE TABLE portrait_lenses (
   433→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   434→    portrait_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES portraits(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   435→    
   436→    -- Lens Identification
   437→    lens_type           lens_type NOT NULL,
   438→    
   439→    -- Lens Data
   440→    analysis            JSONB NOT NULL,  -- Full lens analysis output
   441→    
   442→    -- Key Insights (denormalized for display)
   443→    key_insights        TEXT[],
   444→    primary_pattern     VARCHAR(200),
   445→    
   446→    -- Timestamps
   447→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   448→    
   449→    CONSTRAINT unique_lens UNIQUE (portrait_id, lens_type)
   450→);
   451→
   452→CREATE INDEX idx_lenses_portrait ON portrait_lenses(portrait_id);
   453→```
   454→
   455→## 5.3 Growth Edges
   456→
   457→```sql
   458→CREATE TYPE growth_edge_status AS ENUM (
   459→    'identified',
   460→    'emerging',
   461→    'practicing',
   462→    'integrating',
   463→    'integrated'
   464→);
   465→
   466→CREATE TABLE growth_edges (
   467→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   468→    portrait_id         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES portraits(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   469→    user_id             UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   470→    
   471→    -- Identity
   472→    edge_key            VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'express_needs_directly'
   473→    title               VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
   474→    
   475→    -- Content
   476→    pattern_description TEXT NOT NULL,
   477→    protection_function TEXT,
   478→    cost_description    JSONB,  -- {toSelf, toRelationship, toPartner}
   479→    invitation          TEXT NOT NULL,
   480→    
   481→    -- Practices
   482→    daily_practice      TEXT,
   483→    weekly_practice     TEXT,
   484→    in_moment_practice  TEXT,
   485→    
   486→    -- Anchor
   487→    anchor_phrase       TEXT NOT NULL,
   488→    
   489→    -- Status & Progress
   490→    status              growth_edge_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'identified',
   491→    priority            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   492→    
   493→    -- Tracking
   494→    times_mentioned     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   495→    last_mentioned_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
   496→    
   497→    -- Timestamps
   498→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   499→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   500→    
   501→    CONSTRAINT unique_edge UNIQUE (portrait_id, edge_key)

   809→# 7. Partner & Sharing Entities
   810→
   811→## 7.1 Partnerships
   812→
   813→```sql
   814→CREATE TYPE partnership_status AS ENUM (
   815→    'pending',
   816→    'active',
   817→    'disconnected'
   818→);
   819→
   820→CREATE TABLE partnerships (
   821→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   822→    
   823→    -- Partners (order doesn't matter)
   824→    user_1_id           UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   825→    user_2_id           UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   826→    
   827→    -- Status
   828→    status              partnership_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   829→    
   830→    -- Invitation
   831→    invited_by          UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
   832→    invite_token        VARCHAR(100),
   833→    invite_sent_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
   834→    invite_accepted_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
   835→    
   836→    -- Disconnection
   837→    disconnected_by     UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   838→    disconnected_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
   839→    disconnection_reason VARCHAR(100),
   840→    
   841→    -- Timestamps
   842→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   843→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   844→    
   845→    -- Constraints
   846→    CONSTRAINT different_users CHECK (user_1_id != user_2_id),
   847→    CONSTRAINT ordered_users CHECK (user_1_id < user_2_id),  -- Canonical ordering
   848→    CONSTRAINT unique_partnership UNIQUE (user_1_id, user_2_id)
   849→);
   850→
   851→CREATE INDEX idx_partnerships_user1 ON partnerships(user_1_id);
   852→CREATE INDEX idx_partnerships_user2 ON partnerships(user_2_id);
   853→CREATE INDEX idx_partnerships_active ON partnerships(status) WHERE status = 'active';
   854→CREATE INDEX idx_partnerships_invite ON partnerships(invite_token) WHERE status = 'pending';
   855→```

  1093→# 9. Indexes & Performance
  1094→
  1095→## 9.1 Composite Indexes for Common Queries
  1096→
  1097→```sql
  1098→-- User's active portrait with lenses
  1099→CREATE INDEX idx_portrait_user_active ON portraits(user_id, status) 
  1100→    INCLUDE (snapshot) WHERE status = 'active';
  1101→
  1102→-- Recent sessions with message counts
  1103→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_recent_summary ON chat_sessions(user_id, started_at DESC)
  1104→    INCLUDE (title, message_count, status);
  1105→
  1106→-- Growth edge tracking aggregation
  1107→CREATE INDEX idx_edge_progress_monthly ON growth_edge_progress(growth_edge_id, occurred_at)
  1108→    WHERE occurred_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
  1109→
  1110→-- Pattern frequency tracking
  1111→CREATE INDEX idx_pattern_frequency ON pattern_occurrences(pattern_id, occurred_at DESC);
  1112→```
  1113→
  1114→## 9.2 Partial Indexes
  1115→
  1116→```sql
  1117→-- Only active partnerships
  1118→CREATE INDEX idx_active_partnerships ON partnerships(user_1_id, user_2_id) 
  1119→    WHERE status = 'active';
  1120→
  1121→-- Only non-deleted users
  1122→CREATE INDEX idx_active_users ON users(email) 
  1123→    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND status = 'active';
  1124→
  1125→-- Only completed assessments
  1126→CREATE INDEX idx_completed_assessments ON assessments(user_id, completed_at DESC)
  1127→    WHERE status = 'completed';
  1128→
  1129→-- Only saved anchors
  1130→CREATE INDEX idx_saved_anchors ON anchor_points(user_id, category)
  1131→    WHERE is_saved = TRUE;
  1132→```
  1133→
  1134→## 9.3 JSON Indexes
  1135→
  1136→```sql
  1137→-- Index on attachment style in portrait snapshot
  1138→CREATE INDEX idx_snapshot_attachment ON portraits 
  1139→    USING gin((snapshot->'attachment'));
  1140→
  1141→-- Index on pattern flags
  1142→CREATE INDEX idx_pattern_flags ON detected_patterns 
  1143→    USING gin(flags);
  1144→
  1145→-- Index on message embedded cards
  1146→CREATE INDEX idx_message_cards ON messages

  1149→
  1150→## 9.4 Full-Text Search Indexes
  1151→
  1152→```sql
  1153→-- Search across message content
  1154→CREATE INDEX idx_messages_fts ON messages 
  1155→    USING gin(to_tsvector('english', content))
  1156→    WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
  1157→
  1158→-- Search across session titles
  1159→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_title_fts ON chat_sessions

  1165→
  1166→# 10. Data Lifecycle & Retention
  1167→
  1168→## 10.1 Retention Policies
  1169→
  1170→```sql
  1171→-- Create retention policy table
  1172→CREATE TABLE data_retention_policies (
  1173→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  1174→    table_name          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  1175→    retention_days      INTEGER,  -- NULL = forever
  1176→    soft_delete_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
  1177→    hard_delete_after_days INTEGER,  -- After soft delete
  1178→    archive_enabled     BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  1179→    
  1180→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  1181→    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
  1182→);
  1183→
  1184→-- Insert default policies
  1185→INSERT INTO data_retention_policies (table_name, retention_days, hard_delete_after_days) VALUES
  1186→    ('auth_tokens', 90, 7),
  1187→    ('share_access_log', 365, 30),
  1188→    ('message_metadata', NULL, NULL),  -- Keep forever
  1189→    ('messages', NULL, 30),  -- Soft delete forever, hard delete 30 days after
  1190→    ('daily_checkins', 730, 90),  -- 2 years, then archive
  1191→    ('pattern_occurrences', 730, 90);
  1192→```
  1193→
  1194→## 10.2 Soft Delete Implementation
  1195→
  1196→```sql
  1197→-- Function to soft delete
  1198→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION soft_delete()
  1199→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
  1200→BEGIN
  1201→    NEW.deleted_at = NOW();
  1202→    RETURN NEW;
  1203→END;
  1204→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1205→
  1206→-- Apply to tables with soft delete
  1207→CREATE TRIGGER soft_delete_users
  1208→    BEFORE UPDATE OF deleted_at ON users
  1209→    FOR EACH ROW
  1210→    WHEN (OLD.deleted_at IS NULL AND NEW.deleted_at IS NOT NULL)
  1211→    EXECUTE FUNCTION soft_delete();
  1212→```
  1213→
  1214→## 10.3 Data Export (GDPR/CCPA)
  1215→
  1216→```sql
  1217→-- View for user data export
  1218→CREATE VIEW user_data_export AS
  1219→SELECT 
  1220→    u.id,
  1221→    u.email,
  1222→    u.first_name,
  1223→    u.last_name,
  1224→    u.created_at,
  1225→    (
  1226→        SELECT json_agg(a.*)
  1227→        FROM assessments a
  1228→        WHERE a.user_id = u.id
  1229→    ) as assessments,
  1230→    (
  1231→        SELECT json_agg(p.*)
  1232→        FROM portraits p
  1233→        WHERE p.user_id = u.id
  1234→    ) as portraits,
  1235→    (
  1236→        SELECT json_agg(s.*)
  1237→        FROM chat_sessions s
  1238→        WHERE s.user_id = u.id
  1239→    ) as sessions,
  1240→    (
  1241→        SELECT json_agg(m.*)
  1242→        FROM messages m
  1243→        WHERE m.user_id = u.id
  1244→    ) as messages
  1245→FROM users u;
  1246→
  1247→-- Function to export user data
  1248→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION export_user_data(p_user_id UUID)
  1249→RETURNS JSONB AS $$
  1250→DECLARE
  1251→    result JSONB;
  1252→BEGIN
  1253→    SELECT row_to_json(user_data_export)
  1254→    INTO result
  1255→    FROM user_data_export
  1256→    WHERE id = p_user_id;
  1257→    
  1258→    RETURN result;
  1259→END;
  1260→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1261→```
  1262→
  1263→## 10.4 Data Deletion (GDPR Right to Erasure)
  1264→
  1265→```sql
  1266→-- Function to anonymize/delete user data
  1267→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION delete_user_data(p_user_id UUID)
  1268→RETURNS VOID AS $$
  1269→BEGIN
  1270→    -- Soft delete user
  1271→    UPDATE users 
  1272→    SET 
  1273→        deleted_at = NOW(),
  1274→        email = 'deleted_' || id || '@deleted.local',
  1275→        first_name = 'Deleted',
  1276→        last_name = 'User',
  1277→        password_hash = NULL,
  1278→        phone = NULL
  1279→    WHERE id = p_user_id;
  1280→    
  1281→    -- Remove from partnerships
  1282→    UPDATE partnerships
  1283→    SET 
  1284→        status = 'disconnected',
  1285→        disconnected_at = NOW(),
  1286→        disconnection_reason = 'account_deleted'
  1287→    WHERE user_1_id = p_user_id OR user_2_id = p_user_id;
  1288→    
  1289→    -- Revoke all shares
  1290→    UPDATE partner_shares
  1291→    SET 
  1292→        is_active = FALSE,
  1293→        revoked_at = NOW()
  1294→    WHERE sharer_id = p_user_id OR recipient_id = p_user_id;
  1295→    
  1296→    -- Note: Related data cascades via ON DELETE CASCADE
  1297→    -- or is retained for analytics (anonymized)
  1298→END;
  1299→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1300→```
  1301→
  1302→---
  1303→
  1304→# 11. Security & Encryption
  1305→
  1306→## 11.1 Encryption at Rest
  1307→
  1308→```sql
  1309→-- Enable pgcrypto
  1310→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
  1311→
  1312→-- Encrypted columns approach (for highly sensitive data)
  1313→-- Note: Consider application-level encryption for most cases
  1314→
  1315→-- Example: Encrypting assessment responses
  1316→ALTER TABLE assessment_responses 
  1317→    ADD COLUMN response_value_encrypted BYTEA;
  1318→
  1319→-- Function to encrypt
  1320→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION encrypt_response(p_value JSONB, p_key TEXT)
  1321→RETURNS BYTEA AS $$
  1322→BEGIN
  1323→    RETURN pgp_sym_encrypt(p_value::TEXT, p_key);
  1324→END;
  1325→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1326→
  1327→-- Function to decrypt
  1328→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION decrypt_response(p_encrypted BYTEA, p_key TEXT)
  1329→RETURNS JSONB AS $$
  1330→BEGIN
  1331→    RETURN pgp_sym_decrypt(p_encrypted, p_key)::JSONB;
  1332→END;
  1333→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1334→```
  1335→
  1336→## 11.2 Row-Level Security
  1337→
  1338→```sql
  1339→-- Enable RLS on sensitive tables
  1340→ALTER TABLE portraits ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
  1341→ALTER TABLE messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
  1342→ALTER TABLE assessment_responses ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
  1343→
  1344→-- Policy: Users can only see their own portraits
  1345→CREATE POLICY portraits_user_policy ON portraits
  1346→    FOR ALL
  1347→    USING (user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::UUID);
  1348→
  1349→-- Policy: Users can see shared portraits
  1350→CREATE POLICY portraits_shared_policy ON portraits
  1351→    FOR SELECT
  1352→    USING (
  1353→        EXISTS (
  1354→            SELECT 1 FROM partner_shares ps
  1355→            JOIN partnerships p ON ps.partnership_id = p.id
  1356→            WHERE ps.recipient_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::UUID
  1357→            AND ps.sharer_id = portraits.user_id
  1358→            AND ps.is_active = TRUE
  1359→            AND ps.share_scope = 'full_portrait'
  1360→        )
  1361→    );
  1362→
  1363→-- Policy: Users can only see their own messages
  1364→CREATE POLICY messages_user_policy ON messages
  1365→    FOR ALL
  1366→    USING (user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::UUID);
  1367→```
  1368→
  1369→## 11.3 Audit Logging
  1370→
  1371→```sql
  1372→CREATE TABLE audit_log (
  1373→    id                  UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  1374→    
  1375→    -- Who
  1376→    user_id             UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  1377→    ip_address          INET,
  1378→    user_agent          TEXT,
  1379→    
  1380→    -- What
  1381→    action              VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'create', 'read', 'update', 'delete'
  1382→    table_name          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  1383→    record_id           UUID,
  1384→    
  1385→    -- Details
  1386→    old_values          JSONB,
  1387→    new_values          JSONB,
  1388→    
  1389→    -- When
  1390→    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
  1391→);
  1392→
  1393→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_user ON audit_log(user_id);
  1394→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_table ON audit_log(table_name, record_id);
  1395→CREATE INDEX idx_audit_time ON audit_log(created_at DESC);
  1396→
  1397→-- Trigger function for audit logging
  1398→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_trigger()
  1399→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
  1400→BEGIN
  1401→    INSERT INTO audit_log (
  1402→        user_id,
  1403→        action,
  1404→        table_name,
  1405→        record_id,
  1406→        old_values,
  1407→        new_values
  1408→    ) VALUES (
  1409→        current_setting('app.current_user_id', TRUE)::UUID,
  1410→        TG_OP,
  1411→        TG_TABLE_NAME,
  1412→        COALESCE(NEW.id, OLD.id),
  1413→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('UPDATE', 'DELETE') THEN row_to_json(OLD) END,
  1414→        CASE WHEN TG_OP IN ('INSERT', 'UPDATE') THEN row_to_json(NEW) END
  1415→    );
  1416→    
  1417→    RETURN COALESCE(NEW, OLD);
  1418→END;
  1419→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1420→
  1421→-- Apply audit triggers to sensitive tables
  1422→CREATE TRIGGER audit_portraits
  1423→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON portraits
  1424→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_trigger();
  1425→
  1426→CREATE TRIGGER audit_partner_shares
  1427→    AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON partner_shares
  1428→    FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_trigger();
  1429→```
  1430→

  1481→
  1482→## 12.2 Prisma Schema Example
  1483→
  1484→```prisma
  1485→// schema.prisma
  1486→
  1487→generator client {
  1488→  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  1489→}
  1490→
  1491→datasource db {
  1492→  provider = "postgresql"
  1493→  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
  1494→}
  1495→
  1496→enum UserStatus {
  1497→  active
  1498→  suspended
  1499→  deleted
  1500→}
  1501→
  1502→enum AuthProvider {
  1503→  email
  1504→  google
  1505→  apple
  1506→}
  1507→
  1508→model User {
  1509→  id              String    @id @default(uuid())
  1510→  email           String    @unique
  1511→  emailVerified   Boolean   @default(false)
  1512→  firstName       String
  1513→  lastName        String?
  1514→  displayName     String?
  1515→  avatarUrl       String?
  1516→  timezone        String    @default("UTC")
  1517→  locale          String    @default("en-US")
  1518→  passwordHash    String?
  1519→  authProvider    AuthProvider @default(email)
  1520→  authProviderId  String?
  1521→  status          UserStatus @default(active)
  1522→  lastActiveAt    DateTime?
  1523→  onboardingCompleted Boolean @default(false)
  1524→  onboardingStep  String?
  1525→  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())
  1526→  updatedAt       DateTime  @updatedAt
  1527→  deletedAt       DateTime?
  1528→
  1529→  // Relations
  1530→  preferences     UserPreference?
  1531→  assessments     Assessment[]
  1532→  portraits       Portrait[]
  1533→  sessions        ChatSession[]
  1534→  messages        Message[]
  1535→  growthEdges     GrowthEdge[]
  1536→  anchorPoints    AnchorPoint[]
  1537→  growthTracking  GrowthTracking?
  1538→  
  1539→  @@map("users")
  1540→}
  1541→
  1542→model Assessment {
  1543→  id                String   @id @default(uuid())
  1544→  userId            String
  1545→  status            String   @default("not_started")
  1546→  currentSection    String?
  1547→  currentQuestion   Int      @default(0)
  1548→  sectionsCompleted String[]
  1549→  startedAt         DateTime?
  1550→  completedAt       DateTime?
  1551→  lastActivityAt    DateTime?
  1552→  totalTimeSeconds  Int      @default(0)
  1553→  instrumentVersion String   @default("1.0")
  1554→  scores            Json?
  1555→  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
  1556→  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
  1557→
  1558→  // Relations
  1559→  user              User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  1560→  responses         AssessmentResponse[]
  1561→  sectionScores     AssessmentSectionScore[]
  1562→  portrait          Portrait?
  1563→
  1564→  @@map("assessments")
  1565→}
  1566→
  1567→model Portrait {
  1568→  id                String   @id @default(uuid())
  1569→  userId            String
  1570→  assessmentId      String   @unique
  1571→  status            String   @default("generating")
  1572→  version           Int      @default(1)
  1573→  snapshot          Json
  1574→  narrative         Json?
  1575→  generatedAt       DateTime?
  1576→  generationTimeMs  Int?
  1577→  algorithmVersion  String
  1578→  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())
  1579→  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
  1580→
  1581→  // Relations
  1582→  user              User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  1583→  assessment        Assessment @relation(fields: [assessmentId], references: [id])
  1584→  lenses            PortraitLens[]
  1585→  growthEdges       GrowthEdge[]
  1586→  anchorPoints      AnchorPoint[]
  1587→  negativeCycle     NegativeCycleConfig?
  1588→  partnerGuide      PartnerGuide?
  1589→  patterns          DetectedPattern[]
  1590→
  1591→  @@map("portraits")
  1592→}
  1593→
  1594→// ... Continue for all entities
  1595→```

  1643→# APPENDIX B: Redis Schema
  1644→
  1645→```

  1663→# Job Queues

I need the exact code to understand current patterns. Give me the full file contents for each.

content.text:
Perfect! I have retrieved all seven files with their complete contents. Here's a summary of what you have:

## File Contents Summary

### 1. **app/(app)/home.tsx** (166 lines)
Home screen component for authenticated users. Features:
- Displays user email greeting
- "Start Assessment" button that clears saved progress
- "Continue" button that appears if there's in-progress assessment (Question X of 36)
- Logout button
- Uses AsyncStorage to track assessment progress via `ASSESSMENT_PROGRESS_KEY`
- Auth context for user data and signOut

### 2. **app/(app)/results.tsx** (246 lines)
Results display screen after assessment completion. Features:
- Shows anxiety and avoidance scores (1-7 scale) with progress bars
- Displays attachment style label (from interpretation)
- Shows interpretation text and growth tip from `getInterpretation()`
- Two action buttons: "Back to Home" and "Retake Assessment"
- Calculates percentages for visual progress bars
- Uses route params to receive scores and attachment style

### 3. **app/(app)/_layout.tsx** (12 lines)
Layout for (app) route group:
- Stack navigator with `headerShown: false`
- Declares three screens: home, assessment, results

### 4. **app/_layout.tsx** (17 lines)
Root layout configuration:
- AuthProvider wraps entire app
- Stack navigator with three route groups: index, (auth), (app)
- StatusBar set to dark style
- All headers hidden

### 5. **app/index.tsx** (47 lines)
Root index/splash screen:
- Loading state shows app title with spinner
- Authenticated users redirect to `/(app)/home`
- Unauthenticated users redirect to `/(auth)/login`
- Uses `useAuth()` to check session and loading state

### 6. **utils/questions.ts** (62 lines)
ECR-R Assessment data:
- 36 questions total from Fraley, Shaver & Brennan (2000)
- Items 1-18: Anxiety subscale
- Items 19-36: Avoidance subscale
- 6 reverse-scored items: 20, 21, 30, 33, 34, 35
- Likert scale 1-7 with labels ("Strongly Disagree" to "Strongly Agree")

### 7. **utils/interpretations.ts** (48 lines)
Interpretation framework for four attachment styles:
- **Secure**: comfortable with closeness and independence
- **Anxious-Preoccupied**: values closeness, worries about reciprocation
- **Dismissive-Avoidant**: values independence, uncomfortable with closeness
- **Fearful-Avoidant**: push-pull dynamic with conflicting desires
- Each style has: label, warm interpretation, and growth-oriented tip
- `getInterpretation()` function returns StyleInfo for given attachment style

These files establish a clean pattern: authentication flow → home screen → assessment (not shown but referenced) → results screen with interpretations. The data is separated into utilities, and theming is centralized via constants.

content.text: Now I have a thorough understanding of everything. Let me design the plan.

input.prompt:
You are designing an implementation plan for adding 5 more psychological assessments to an existing React Native (Expo Router) couples app. The app currently has ECR-R (36 items) working. We need to add: IPIP-NEO-120 (120 items), Custom Values (29 scaled + 3 open + 8 scenarios + ranking), SSEIT (33 items), DUTCH (20 items), DSI-R (46 items).

## CURRENT ARCHITECTURE

The app uses:
- Expo Router with file-based routing
- Supabase for auth + DB
- AsyncStorage for in-progress assessment persistence
- Pattern: utils/questions.ts + utils/scoring.ts + utils/interpretations.ts

Current files that need to change:
- `app/(app)/_layout.tsx` - Only has routes for: home, assessment, results
- `app/(app)/home.tsx` - Hardcoded to single ECR-R assessment, checks `ecr_r_progress` key
- `app/(app)/assessment.tsx` - Hardcoded: imports ECR_R_QUESTIONS, LIKERT_LABELS, calculateECRRScores; TOTAL_QUESTIONS=36; saves to `ecr_r_assessments` table
- `app/(app)/results.tsx` - Hardcoded to show anxietyScore/avoidanceScore/attachmentStyle
- `utils/questions.ts` - Only ECR-R questions (36 items, 7-point Likert)
- `utils/scoring.ts` - Only calculateECRRScores function
- `utils/interpretations.ts` - Only attachment style interpretations
- `types/index.ts` - Only ECR-R types, AssessmentType = 'ecr-r'

## USER DECISIONS
1. Generalize assessment infrastructure FIRST (Phase A)
2. Use ONE `assessments` table with a `type` field + JSONB for scores
3. Add section breaks/pauses for long assessments (especially IPIP-NEO-120)
4. Build Values instrument LAST (most complex UI)
5. Let users pick assessments freely (or skip)

## ASSESSMENT DETAILS

### DUTCH (20 items, 5-pt Likert, NO reverse scoring)
- 5 subscales: Yielding, Compromising, Forcing, Problem-Solving, Avoiding (4 items each)
- Output: subscale means + primary/secondary style
- Simplest assessment to add

### SSEIT (33 items, 5-pt Likert, 3 reverse items: 5,28,33)
- 4 subscales: Perception(14), Managing Own(4), Managing Others(5), Utilization(10)
- Output: subscale means, total, normalized to 0-100
- Straightforward Likert

### DSI-R (46 items, 6-pt Likert, 12 reverse items)
- 4 subscales: Emotional Reactivity(11), I-Position(11), Emotional Cutoff(12), Fusion with Others(12)
- SPECIAL: After reverse-scoring items, subscale means are REVERSED again (7 - mean) so high = differentiated
- Output: subscale means (reversed), total mean, normalized 0-100

### IPIP-NEO-120 (120 items, 5-pt Likert, 50+ reverse items)
- 5 domains (24 items each), 30 facets (4 items each)
- Need section breaks every 24 items (1 domain)
- Output: domain sums/means, facet sums/means, percentiles via z-score approximation

### Custom Values (MOST COMPLEX - 3 different response types)
- Part 1: 20 scaled items (1-10 Likert, paired importance/accordance) + ranking of top 5
- Part 2: 3 open text responses (500-750 char limit)
- Part 3: 8 scenario-based multiple choice items (4 options each, coded)
- Output: domain scores with gap calculation, rankings, qualitative responses, action coding, avoidance tendency

## DESIGN REQUIREMENTS

1. Generic AssessmentConfig type that describes any assessment
2. Generic assessment screen that adapts to the config (different Likert scales, text input, choice input, section breaks)
3. Single `assessments` Supabase table with type field + JSONB
4. Assessment hub/picker on home screen showing all 6 assessments with completion status
5. Per-assessment results screens (each assessment has different output to display)
6. Assessment order is user's choice, can skip

Please design a detailed, phased implementation plan. For each phase, specify:
- Exact files to create/modify
- What each file should contain (key interfaces, functions, components)
- The order to implement things
- How to handle the different question types (Likert with different scales, text input, scenario choice, ranking)

Focus on pragmatic, minimal-but-extensible design. Don't over-engineer.

Yes, exactly. I'm reading the specs and research from `/Users/shaharrabi/Desktop/AI projects TOM /couples-app-v0.1/archived-specs/` (that's where all the assessment specifications, the big technical report, and the research docs live), and then implementing everything in the actual project at `/Users/shaharrabi/couples-app-v0.1/` (which is our working codebase with the Expo app).

input.content:
# Plan: Generalize Assessment Infrastructure + Add 5 Assessments

## Overview

Refactor the hardcoded ECR-R assessment into a generic assessment engine, then add 5 more assessments one at a time. Users can pick assessments freely or skip.

**Source specs:** `/Users/shaharrabi/Desktop/AI projects TOM /couples-app-v0.1/archived-specs/assessment-instruments-specification-complete.md`
**Working codebase:** `/Users/shaharrabi/couples-app-v0.1/`

---

## Phase A: Generalize Infrastructure

### A1. Expand type system
**Modify:** `types/index.ts`
- Add `AssessmentType` union: `'ecr-r' | 'ipip-neo-120' | 'values' | 'sseit' | 'dutch' | 'dsi-r'`
- Add `GenericQuestion` interface with `inputType: 'likert' | 'text' | 'choice' | 'ranking'`
- Add `AssessmentSection` interface (for section breaks)
- Add `AssessmentConfig` interface (name, questions, likertScale, sections, scoringFn, progressKey)
- Add `AssessmentRecord` for the unified DB table
- Add score types: `DUTCHScores`, `SSEITScores`, `DSIRScores`, `IPIPScores`, `ValuesScores`
- Keep existing `ECRRScores`, `AttachmentStyle`

### A2. Create assessment registry
**New:** `utils/assessments/registry.ts`
- `ASSESSMENT_REGISTRY: Record<AssessmentType, AssessmentConfig>`
- `getAssessmentConfig(type)` and `getAllAssessments()` functions
- Display order array

### A3. Migrate ECR-R into config pattern
**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/ecr-r.ts`
- Move questions from `utils/questions.ts` into `GenericQuestion[]` format
- Move scoring from `utils/scoring.ts` into config's `scoringFn`
- Move interpretations from `utils/interpretations.ts`
- Export `ecrRConfig: AssessmentConfig`

### A4. Create unified Supabase table
**Manual:** Run SQL in Supabase dashboard
```sql
CREATE TABLE assessments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,
  responses JSONB NOT NULL,
  scores JSONB NOT NULL,
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_assessments_user_type ON assessments(user_id, type, completed_at DESC);
ALTER TABLE assessments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users can manage own assessments" ON assessments FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

### A5. Refactor assessment screen to be generic
**Modify:** `app/(app)/assessment.tsx` (major rewrite)
- Read `type` from route params → `getAssessmentConfig(type)`
- Use config for: questions, likertScale, totalQuestions, progressKey
- Render different input components based on `question.inputType`
- Add section break logic (interstitial between sections)
- On submit: call `config.scoringFn()`, save to unified `assessments` table
- Navigate to results with `type` + `scores` params

### A6. Refactor home screen to assessment hub
**Modify:** `app/(app)/home.tsx` (major rewrite)
- Show all assessments as cards (name, description, time, status)
- Query Supabase for completion status per assessment type
- Check AsyncStorage for in-progress state per assessment
- Status badges: "Not Started", "In Progress (Q X/Y)", "Completed"
- Tap card → navigate to `/(app)/assessment?type=xxx`

### A7. Refactor results screen
**Modify:** `app/(app)/results.tsx`
- Read `type` param, delegate to per-assessment results component
- **New:** `components/results/ECRRResults.tsx` (extract current results UI)

### A8. Add section break component
**New:** `components/assessment/SectionBreak.tsx`
- Shows section title, description, progress so far
- "Continue" and "Save & Exit" buttons

**CHECKPOINT: ECR-R works through the new generic system before proceeding**

---

## Phase B: DUTCH (20 items — simplest, validates the generic engine)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/dutch.ts`
- 20 questions, 5-pt Likert (Never→Always), NO reverse scoring
- 5 subscales: Yielding, Compromising, Forcing, Problem-Solving, Avoiding (4 items each)
- Scoring: subscale means, primary/secondary style detection

**New:** `components/results/DUTCHResults.tsx`
- 5 subscale bars, primary/secondary style labels, interpretation text

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/dutch.ts`

Register in registry → test end-to-end.

---

## Phase C: SSEIT (33 items — straightforward Likert)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/sseit.ts`
- 33 questions, 5-pt Likert, 3 reverse items (5, 28, 33)
- 4 subscales: Perception(14), Managing Own(4), Managing Others(5), Utilization(10)
- Scoring: reverse via `6 - score`, subscale means, normalize to 0-100

**New:** `components/results/SSEITResults.tsx`
- Total EI score (0-100), 4 subscale bars, level labels

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/sseit.ts`

---

## Phase D: DSI-R (46 items — double-reversal scoring)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/dsi-r.ts`
- 46 questions, 6-pt Likert, 12 reverse items
- 4 subscales: Emotional Reactivity(11), I-Position(11), Emotional Cutoff(12), Fusion with Others(12)
- **Special scoring:** reverse items via `7 - score`, then reverse subscale means via `7 - rawMean` (so high = differentiated), normalize to 0-100

**New:** `components/results/DSIRResults.tsx`
- Total differentiation (0-100), 4 subscale bars, descriptions

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/dsi-r.ts`

---

## Phase E: IPIP-NEO-120 (120 items — longest, section breaks)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/ipip-neo-120.ts` (large file)
- 120 questions, 5-pt Likert, 50+ reverse items
- 5 domains × 24 items, 30 facets × 4 items
- 5 sections with breaks every 24 items (one per domain)
- Scoring: reverse via `6 - score`, facet sums/means, domain sums/means, percentiles via logistic CDF approximation

**New:** `components/results/IPIPResults.tsx`
- 5 domain bars with percentiles
- Expandable domain sections showing 6 facet scores each
- Level labels (Very Low → Very High)

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/ipip-neo-120.ts`

---

## Phase F: Custom Values (most complex — 3 response types)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/values.ts`
- Part 1: 20 scaled items (1-10 Likert, importance/accordance pairs) + 1 ranking exercise
- Part 2: 3 open text responses (charLimit: 500-750)
- Part 3: 8 scenario-based choice items (4 options each)
- 3 sections with breaks between parts
- Scoring: gap scores, rankings, action coding, avoidance/balanced tendency

**New:** `components/assessment/RankingInput.tsx`
- Tap-to-select-in-order (tap 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th from 10 domains) — simpler than drag

**New:** `components/assessment/ChoiceInput.tsx`
- 4 option cards (A, B, C, D) with full text

**New:** `components/results/ValuesResults.tsx`
- Domain bars (importance vs accordance with gap highlighting)
- Top 5 ranked values
- High-gap domains called out
- Scenario response patterns
- Qualitative responses as read-only cards

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/values.ts`

---

## New/Modified File Summary

```
MODIFY:
  types/index.ts                         — expanded type system
  app/(app)/assessment.tsx               — generic assessment engine
  app/(app)/home.tsx                     — assessment hub
  app/(app)/results.tsx                  — type-based delegation

NEW:
  utils/assessments/registry.ts          — central registry
  utils/assessments/configs/ecr-r.ts     — ECR-R config
  utils/assessments/configs/dutch.ts     — DUTCH config
  utils/assessments/configs/sseit.ts     — SSEIT config
  utils/assessments/configs/dsi-r.ts     — DSI-R config
  utils/assessments/configs/ipip-neo-120.ts — IPIP config
  utils/assessments/configs/values.ts    — Values config
  utils/assessments/interpretations/ecr-r.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/dutch.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/sseit.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/dsi-r.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/ipip-neo-120.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/values.ts
```

# Plan: Generalize Assessment Infrastructure + Add 5 Assessments

## Overview

Refactor the hardcoded ECR-R assessment into a generic assessment engine, then add 5 more assessments one at a time. Users can pick assessments freely or skip.

**Source specs:** `/Users/shaharrabi/Desktop/AI projects TOM /couples-app-v0.1/archived-specs/assessment-instruments-specification-complete.md`
**Working codebase:** `/Users/shaharrabi/couples-app-v0.1/`

---

## Phase A: Generalize Infrastructure

### A1. Expand type system
**Modify:** `types/index.ts`
- Add `AssessmentType` union: `'ecr-r' | 'ipip-neo-120' | 'values' | 'sseit' | 'dutch' | 'dsi-r'`
- Add `GenericQuestion` interface with `inputType: 'likert' | 'text' | 'choice' | 'ranking'`
- Add `AssessmentSection` interface (for section breaks)
- Add `AssessmentConfig` interface (name, questions, likertScale, sections, scoringFn, progressKey)
- Add `AssessmentRecord` for the unified DB table
- Add score types: `DUTCHScores`, `SSEITScores`, `DSIRScores`, `IPIPScores`, `ValuesScores`
- Keep existing `ECRRScores`, `AttachmentStyle`

### A2. Create assessment registry
**New:** `utils/assessments/registry.ts`
- `ASSESSMENT_REGISTRY: Record<AssessmentType, AssessmentConfig>`
- `getAssessmentConfig(type)` and `getAllAssessments()` functions
- Display order array

### A3. Migrate ECR-R into config pattern
**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/ecr-r.ts`
- Move questions from `utils/questions.ts` into `GenericQuestion[]` format
- Move scoring from `utils/scoring.ts` into config's `scoringFn`
- Move interpretations from `utils/interpretations.ts`
- Export `ecrRConfig: AssessmentConfig`

### A4. Create unified Supabase table
**Manual:** Run SQL in Supabase dashboard
```sql
CREATE TABLE assessments (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,
  responses JSONB NOT NULL,
  scores JSONB NOT NULL,
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_assessments_user_type ON assessments(user_id, type, completed_at DESC);
ALTER TABLE assessments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "Users can manage own assessments" ON assessments FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

### A5. Refactor assessment screen to be generic
**Modify:** `app/(app)/assessment.tsx` (major rewrite)
- Read `type` from route params → `getAssessmentConfig(type)`
- Use config for: questions, likertScale, totalQuestions, progressKey
- Render different input components based on `question.inputType`
- Add section break logic (interstitial between sections)
- On submit: call `config.scoringFn()`, save to unified `assessments` table
- Navigate to results with `type` + `scores` params

### A6. Refactor home screen to assessment hub
**Modify:** `app/(app)/home.tsx` (major rewrite)
- Show all assessments as cards (name, description, time, status)
- Query Supabase for completion status per assessment type
- Check AsyncStorage for in-progress state per assessment
- Status badges: "Not Started", "In Progress (Q X/Y)", "Completed"
- Tap card → navigate to `/(app)/assessment?type=xxx`

### A7. Refactor results screen
**Modify:** `app/(app)/results.tsx`
- Read `type` param, delegate to per-assessment results component
- **New:** `components/results/ECRRResults.tsx` (extract current results UI)

### A8. Add section break component
**New:** `components/assessment/SectionBreak.tsx`
- Shows section title, description, progress so far
- "Continue" and "Save & Exit" buttons

**CHECKPOINT: ECR-R works through the new generic system before proceeding**

---

## Phase B: DUTCH (20 items — simplest, validates the generic engine)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/dutch.ts`
- 20 questions, 5-pt Likert (Never→Always), NO reverse scoring
- 5 subscales: Yielding, Compromising, Forcing, Problem-Solving, Avoiding (4 items each)
- Scoring: subscale means, primary/secondary style detection

**New:** `components/results/DUTCHResults.tsx`
- 5 subscale bars, primary/secondary style labels, interpretation text

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/dutch.ts`

Register in registry → test end-to-end.

---

## Phase C: SSEIT (33 items — straightforward Likert)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/sseit.ts`
- 33 questions, 5-pt Likert, 3 reverse items (5, 28, 33)
- 4 subscales: Perception(14), Managing Own(4), Managing Others(5), Utilization(10)
- Scoring: reverse via `6 - score`, subscale means, normalize to 0-100

**New:** `components/results/SSEITResults.tsx`
- Total EI score (0-100), 4 subscale bars, level labels

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/sseit.ts`

---

## Phase D: DSI-R (46 items — double-reversal scoring)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/dsi-r.ts`
- 46 questions, 6-pt Likert, 12 reverse items
- 4 subscales: Emotional Reactivity(11), I-Position(11), Emotional Cutoff(12), Fusion with Others(12)
- **Special scoring:** reverse items via `7 - score`, then reverse subscale means via `7 - rawMean` (so high = differentiated), normalize to 0-100

**New:** `components/results/DSIRResults.tsx`
- Total differentiation (0-100), 4 subscale bars, descriptions

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/dsi-r.ts`

---

## Phase E: IPIP-NEO-120 (120 items — longest, section breaks)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/ipip-neo-120.ts` (large file)
- 120 questions, 5-pt Likert, 50+ reverse items
- 5 domains × 24 items, 30 facets × 4 items
- 5 sections with breaks every 24 items (one per domain)
- Scoring: reverse via `6 - score`, facet sums/means, domain sums/means, percentiles via logistic CDF approximation

**New:** `components/results/IPIPResults.tsx`
- 5 domain bars with percentiles
- Expandable domain sections showing 6 facet scores each
- Level labels (Very Low → Very High)

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/ipip-neo-120.ts`

---

## Phase F: Custom Values (most complex — 3 response types)

**New:** `utils/assessments/configs/values.ts`
- Part 1: 20 scaled items (1-10 Likert, importance/accordance pairs) + 1 ranking exercise
- Part 2: 3 open text responses (charLimit: 500-750)
- Part 3: 8 scenario-based choice items (4 options each)
- 3 sections with breaks between parts
- Scoring: gap scores, rankings, action coding, avoidance/balanced tendency

**New:** `components/assessment/RankingInput.tsx`
- Tap-to-select-in-order (tap 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th from 10 domains) — simpler than drag

**New:** `components/assessment/ChoiceInput.tsx`
- 4 option cards (A, B, C, D) with full text

**New:** `components/results/ValuesResults.tsx`
- Domain bars (importance vs accordance with gap highlighting)
- Top 5 ranked values
- High-gap domains called out
- Scenario response patterns
- Qualitative responses as read-only cards

**New:** `utils/assessments/interpretations/values.ts`

---

## New/Modified File Summary

```
MODIFY:
  types/index.ts                         — expanded type system
  app/(app)/assessment.tsx               — generic assessment engine
  app/(app)/home.tsx                     — assessment hub
  app/(app)/results.tsx                  — type-based delegation

NEW:
  utils/assessments/registry.ts          — central registry
  utils/assessments/configs/ecr-r.ts     — ECR-R config
  utils/assessments/configs/dutch.ts     — DUTCH config
  utils/assessments/configs/sseit.ts     — SSEIT config
  utils/assessments/configs/dsi-r.ts     — DSI-R config
  utils/assessments/configs/ipip-neo-120.ts — IPIP config
  utils/assessments/configs/values.ts    — Values config
  utils/assessments/interpretations/ecr-r.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/dutch.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/sseit.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/dsi-r.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/ipip-neo-120.ts
  utils/assessments/interpretations/values.ts
  components/results/ECRRResults.tsx
  components/results/DUTCHResults.tsx
  components/results/SSEITResults.tsx
  components/results/DSIRResults.tsx
  components/results/IPIPResults.tsx
  components/results/ValuesResults.tsx
  components/assessment/SectionBreak.tsx
  components/assessment/RankingInput.tsx  (Phase F only)
  components/assessment/ChoiceInput.tsx   (Phase F only)
```

---

## Implementation Order

| Step | What | Effort |
|------|------|--------|
| A1 | Expand types | Small |
| A2 | Create registry | Small |
| A3 | ECR-R config | Medium |
| A4 | Supabase table (manual) | Small |
| A5 | Generic assessment screen | **Large** |
| A6 | Assessment hub home screen | Medium |
| A7 | Results refactor + ECRRResults | Medium |
| A8 | SectionBreak component | Small |
| **✓** | **ECR-R works through new system** | |
| B | DUTCH (config + results + register) | Medium |
| **✓** | **DUTCH works end-to-end** | |
| C | SSEIT | Medium |
| D | DSI-R | Medium |
| E | IPIP-NEO-120 (+ section breaks test) | Large |
| F | Values (+ ranking/choice UI) | **Large** |

---

## Verification Plan

After each phase:
1. Run `npx expo start` — app launches without errors
2. Navigate to home — see assessment cards with correct status
3. Start the new assessment — questions render with correct scale
4. Answer all questions — progress saves/restores via AsyncStorage
5. Submit — scores compute correctly, save to `assessments` table
6. Results screen — displays correct subscales/interpretations
7. Retake — can retake; new record created
8. Previously completed ECR-R still works

For IPIP (Phase E): verify section breaks appear between domains.

content.text:
The plan is written. To summarize the approach:

**6 phases, building incrementally:**
- **Phase A** — Generalize the infrastructure: expand types, create an assessment registry, migrate ECR-R into the new config pattern, create unified Supabase table, make the assessment screen generic, build the assessment hub on home
- **Phase B** — DUTCH (20 items, simplest — validates the engine works)
- **Phase C** — SSEIT (33 items, straightforward)
- **Phase D** — DSI-R (46 items, tricky double-reversal scoring)
- **Phase E** — IPIP-NEO-120 (120 items, section breaks every 24 questions)
- **Phase F** — Values (most complex: Likert + text + scenario choices + ranking)

Each phase ends with a checkpoint where we verify everything works before moving on. ~25 new files, 4 modified files.

run npm install for new dependencies
create directory structure for new files

```sql
CREATE POLICY "Users can manage own assessments" ON assessments FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

### A5. Refactor assessment screen to be generic

- Status badges: "Not Started", "In Progress (Q X/Y)", "Completed"

## Phase B: DUTCH (20 items — simplest, validates the generic engine)

Refactor the hardcoded ECR-R assessment into a generic assessment engine, then add 5 more assessments one at a time. Users can pick assessments freely or skip.

### A1. Expand type system
**Modify:** `types/index.ts`
- Add `AssessmentType` union: `'ecr-r' | 'ipip-neo-120' | 'values' | 'sseit' | 'dutch' | 'dsi-r'`
- Add `GenericQuestion` interface with `inputType: 'likert' | 'text' | 'choice' | 'ranking'`
